elements.text: Freddie Mac provides to the Servicer the three monthly reports described below that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must use to reconcile the amounts reported to Freddie Mac and drafted by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac from the Servicer’s designated Custodial Account. The Servicer must review the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following reports timely and notify Freddie Mac in writing (see Directory 7) of any errors or
elements.label: text

elements.text: discrepancies between its data and the report:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Loan Reconciliation Difference Report: Freddie Mac will make available to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer the final Loan Reconciliation Difference Report through the Freddie Mac Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Level Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88) on the second Business Day of the month following
elements.label: text

elements.text: the end of the Accounting Cycle. This report provides the Servicer with detailed
elements.label: text

elements.text: information about transactions that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac processed differently than the Servicer reported
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac added manually
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer reported as revisions that Freddie Mac did not process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer reported as revisions that Freddie Mac did process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer reported more than once during the Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer reported with an invalid Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer reported on Mortgages belonging to another Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The differences identified on this report may be outstanding items on the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated Custodial Account reconciliation or adjustments that the Servicer needs to
elements.label: text

elements.text: make to the Servicer’s cash remittance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Monthly Account Statement (MAS): Freddie Mac will make available to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: the final MAS through the Loan Level Reporting tool on the second Business Day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month following the end of the Accounting Cycle. This report summarizes the total
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount due to Freddie Mac as of the Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The ending balance on the MAS includes principal and interest due to Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts drafted from the Servicer and adjustments made by Freddie Mac. The ending
elements.label: text

elements.text: balance is the amount outstanding (pending Freddie Mac’s draft) and non-sufficient
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds, if applicable, due to Freddie Mac as of the end of the Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must reconcile the ending balance to the Principal and Interest Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account balance using Form 59, Principal and Interest Custodial Account Reconciliation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Worksheet—Monthly Account Statement, in accordance with Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Sections 8304.4(a) through 8304.4(e). If the Servicer disagrees with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: ending balance on the MAS, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac in writing (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 7) within 90 days of the end of the Accounting Cycle. Call Customer Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: at 800-FREDDIE for further information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Draft Report: The Draft Report will provide summary and loan-level detail of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts due and offsetting adjustments associated with revisions, based on Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting. A preliminary Draft Report will be available in the Cash Manager tool (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88) and updated based on daily reporting. A final version of the Draft Report for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the current Accounting Cycle will be available in the Cash Manager tool on the morning
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the P&I Determination Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Draft Report details are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the principal and forecasted scheduled interest are reported on or prior to the P&I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determination Date, then:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The principal will be reflected on the Draft Report for the current Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The forecasted scheduled interest will be reflected on the Draft Report for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: next Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the principal and forecasted scheduled interest are reported after the P&I
elements.label: text

elements.text: Determination Date, then the principal and forecasted scheduled interest amounts will
elements.label: text

elements.text: be reflected on the Draft Report for the next Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a principal and interest revision in the current Accounting Cycle is reported on or
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the P&I Determination Date, then:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An offsetting principal adjustment will be reflected on the same day as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: original transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ An offsetting interest adjustment will be reflected on the same day as the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a principal and interest revision is reported after the P&I Determination Date, then
elements.label: text

elements.text: an offsetting principal and interest adjustment will be reflected on the Draft Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the next Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a payoff is reported by the Payoff Determination Date, the payoff transaction will
elements.label: text

elements.text: be reflected on the Draft Report and drafted on the fifth Business Day after the Payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a payoff is reported after the Payoff Determination Date, the payoff transaction will
elements.label: text

elements.text: be reflected on the Draft Report and drafted on the second Business Day after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff transaction is successfully reported to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a payoff is revised prior to the Payoff Determination Date, an offsetting adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the previously accepted payoff amount will be reflected on the Draft Report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a payoff is revised after the Payoff Determination Date, an offsetting adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: will be reflected on the Draft Report on the second Business Day after the payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: revision is successfully reported to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If an REO was reported and the Mortgage was inactive as of the previous Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle, a credit for the reimbursement of advanced interest (calculated from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: DDLPI up to, but not including, the month the Servicer inactivated the Mortgage), if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, will be reflected in the current Accounting Cycle Draft Report if reported
elements.label: text

elements.text: as REO on or before the P&I Determination Date, or the next Accounting Cycle if
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported as REO after the P&I Determination Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If an REO was reported and the Mortgage was active as of the previous Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle, a credit for the reimbursement of advanced interest (calculated from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: DDLPI through the end of the previous Accounting Cycle), if applicable, will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reflected in the current Accounting Cycle Draft Report if reported as REO on or
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the P&I Determination Date, or the next Accounting Cycle if reported as REO
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the P&I Determination Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Reports to reconcile monthly activity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will provide preliminary and final versions of the reports listed in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.4(d) as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Preliminary
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer reports and resolves all data discrepancies prior to the close of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle, the preliminary Loan Reconciliation Difference Report will be
elements.label: text

elements.text: available in the Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88), and the Draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report will be available through the Freddie Mac Cash Manager tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may use the preliminary reconciliation reports to begin reconciling its
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly Mortgage activity and Principal and Interest Custodial Account or use them to
elements.label: text

elements.text: make revisions to the Servicer’s loan-level transactions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Final reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: After Freddie Mac closes its Accounting Cycle, Freddie Mac will provide the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: the final MAS and the Loan Reconciliation Difference Reports through the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Level Reporting tool.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will provide the final Draft Report to the Servicer through the Cash
elements.label: text

elements.text: Manager tool on the second Business Day of the month following the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer should use these reports to reconcile its investor reporting and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s Custodial Account activity for the month in accordance with Chapter 8304.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.5: Noncompliance with reporting standards and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: compensatory fees (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Noncompliance with reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Drafting noncompliance compensatory fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reporting noncompliance compensatory fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ EDR noncompliance compensatory fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unreported transactions and loan simulation compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Aged data errors compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Next month late reported payoff noncompliance compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Draft delay compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contract noncompliance and contract change compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Noncompliance with reporting and drafting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer’s failure to comply with Freddie Mac’s requirements for reporting and drafting
elements.label: text

elements.text: constitutes a violation of the Purchase Documents and the contractual obligations of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide. In addition to any other remedies Freddie Mac may have at law or in equity, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is subject to the assessment of compensatory fees for any violations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will charge the Servicer a noncompliance compensatory fee for the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: failure to comply with Freddie Mac’s Servicing reporting requirements contained in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8106.1 or the accounting reporting time requirements contained in this chapter. Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting and accounting reporting noncompliance compensatory fees are each monitored
elements.label: text

elements.text: and assessed separately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer will receive written notification of all compensatory and other fee assessments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to revise Freddie Mac’s compensatory and other fee schedules
elements.label: text

elements.text: and change Freddie Mac’s methods of calculating compensatory and other fees at any time at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s sole discretion. The Servicer may direct any questions regarding the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessment of accounting reporting compensatory and other fees to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 7).
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 8303.5(b) for instructions on remitting compensatory fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must complete, execute and submit to Freddie Mac Form 1132, Authorization
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Automatic Transfer of Funds Through the Automated Clearing House (ACH) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers, as an Electronic Record (as defined in Section 1401.1(b)), using a Portable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document Format (PDF) (or other Electronic Record format commonly used in the mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: industry), that has been completed and contains the copy or representation of the pen and ink
elements.label: text

elements.text: signature of the Servicer’s Authorized Employee (such copy or representation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Employee’s signature shall be such Authorized Employee’s adopted Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature as defined in Section 1401.1(b)) and either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Attach to an e-mail and deliver to Freddie Mac at CashCollections@FreddieMac.com;
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upload through the Freddie Mac eBill system
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and the Servicer agree that the delivery of Electronic Records with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Signatures are eligible Electronic Transactions (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.1(b)) and are governed by the applicable provisions of Chapter 1401.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The employee authorized to execute Form 1132 on the Servicer’s behalf must be designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: as an “Authorized Employee” on the Servicer’s Form 988SF, Certificate of Incumbency for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bank, Savings Bank, Savings and Loan Association, Credit Union, Corporation or Limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: Liability Company, or Form 989SF, Certificate of Incumbency for Limited Partnerships, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The account identified in Form 1132 for the payment of fees must be a Demand Deposit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account that is separate from any Custodial Account required to be maintained pursuant to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any of the Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer agrees to notify Freddie Mac immediately at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer_Billing@FreddieMac.com of any changes to the status of the Servicer’s ACH
elements.label: text

elements.text: account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Drafting noncompliance compensatory fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will initiate an ACH draft to collect any compensatory fees billed on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Performing Loans monthly Servicing Billing Statement and the Non-Performing Loans
elements.label: text

elements.text: Invoice. To authorize ACH drafting of compensatory fees, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Submit a completed, executed and duly authorized Form 1132 no later than 15 Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days before the last Business Day of the first month in which the Servicer is liable to pay
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac any compensatory fees, to set up or make certain changes to the draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: account. This form will authorize Freddie Mac to initiate an ACH draft and designates an
elements.label: text

elements.text: account from which Freddie Mac will collect any compensatory and other fees for which
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may bill the Servicer on the Performing Loans monthly Servicer Billing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Statement and the Non-Performing Loans Invoice.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Designate an account other than one of Freddie Mac’s Custodial Accounts from which
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will draft the amount due; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Deliver Form 1132 to Freddie Mac as an Electronic Record (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.1(b)), using a Portable Document Format (PDF) (or other Electronic Record format
elements.label: text

elements.text: commonly used in the mortgage industry), that has been completed and contains the copy
elements.label: text

elements.text: or representation of the pen and ink signature of the Servicer’s Authorized Employee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (such copy or representation of the Authorized Employee’s signature shall be such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authorized Employee’s adopted Electronic Signature as defined in Section 1401.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: and either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Attach to an e-mail and deliver to Freddie Mac at the following e-mail address:
elements.label: text

elements.text: CashCollections@FreddieMac.com; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upload through the Freddie Mac eBill system
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac and the Servicer agree that the delivery of Electronic Records with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Signatures are eligible Electronic Transactions and are governed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable provisions of Chapter 1401
elements.label: text

elements.text: The employee authorized to execute Form 1132 on the Servicer’s behalf must be designated
elements.label: text

elements.text: as an “Authorized Employee” on the Servicer’s Form 988SF or Form 989SF, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may establish one ACH account for the payment of invoices for both the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Performing Loans monthly Servicer Billing Statement and the Servicer Non-Performing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loans Invoice or establish two separate accounts. Servicers may submit billing inquires or
elements.label: text

elements.text: billing-related requests to Servicer_Billing@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Section 9102.1 concerning Non-Performing Loans remittances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Sections 7101.1(b), 8303.5(a), 8303.5(c) through 8303.5(i) and 9603.3(f) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on these compensatory fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Reporting noncompliance compensatory fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac assesses a compensatory fee when the Servicer fails to comply with Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s Servicing reporting requirements for the reports listed in Section 8106.1 or with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s reporting requirements contained in this chapter and Section 9102.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer commits more than one type of reporting offense within any consecutive 12-
elements.label: text

elements.text: month period, the Servicer may be subject to more than one reporting noncompliance fee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer will be subject to the applicable compensatory fee in accordance with Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8303.5(d) for its failure to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Submit complete and accurate Servicing reports, including the submission of error-free
elements.label: text

elements.text: loan-level data within the required time frame
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Report mandated data elements, such as the Last Payment Received Date (LPRD) or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other fields as listed in Exhibit 60, Loan-Level Reporting Data Description
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Provide information requested by Freddie Mac that is outside of the regular monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting requirements of this chapter, or its failure to provide such requested information
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a usable form within the required time frame
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will notify the Servicer in writing of all instances of noncompliance.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: month in accordance with Section 9102.6. The compensatory fees are based on the number of
elements.label: text

elements.text: noncompliance violations the Servicer commits in any consecutive 12-month period starting
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the month in which the first violation occurred and as shown below:

elements.text: (e) Unreported transactions and loan simulation compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac assesses a compensatory fee of $100 per loan when a Servicer fails to clear
elements.label: text

elements.text: outstanding edits or report loan-level activity by the end of the Accounting Cycle.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Aged data errors compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may assess a $100 compensatory fee per loan, per occurrence, up to a maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: of $15,000 per month in which there are unresolved loan-level reporting errors that appear on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Loan Reconciliation Difference Report 90 days or more category.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will submit an ACH draft transaction for all data error compensatory fees the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer owes Freddie Mac on the last Business Day of the month in which the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: received the Performing Loans monthly Servicer Billing Statement from Freddie Mac stating
elements.label: text

elements.text: the amount of the compensatory fees.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 8303.5(b) for information on establishing the ACH draft.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Next month late reported payoff noncompliance compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac assesses this compensatory fee when the Servicer fails to report a paid off
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage by the second Business Day of the month following the month in which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer receives the funds. This compensatory fee is equal to one month’s interest based on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the net yield for each loan that the Servicer reports late. Freddie Mac may assess this
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee in addition to other fees contained in this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may assess a late reported payoff compensatory fee if the Servicer fails to report
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payoff of a matured Mortgage in accordance with Section 8301.4(g).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will submit an ACH transaction for all late reported payoff noncompliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fees the Servicer owes Freddie Mac on the last Business Day of the month in
elements.label: text

elements.text: which the Servicer received the Performing Loans monthly Servicer Billing Statement from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac indicating the amount of the compensatory fee. Refer to Section 8303.5(b) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on establishing the ACH draft.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Draft delay compensatory fee
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Compensatory fee with respect to payoff reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to payoffs, Freddie Mac assesses this compensatory fee when the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: fails to report a payoff within two Business Days of the Payoff Date and the late report
elements.label: text

elements.text: results in a delayed Payoff Draft Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: If the Servicer fails to report a paid-off Mortgage by the second Business Day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the month following the month in which the Servicer receives the funds, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: compensatory fee is determined as provided in Section 8303.5(g) rather than this Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Compensatory fee calculation method
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac calculates this compensatory fee by the amount of the payoff funds,
elements.label: text

elements.text: multiplied by the number of calendar days the payoff draft was late as a result of the late
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported payoff, multiplied by the highest quoted prime rate on the last Business Day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the month in which the late payoff draft occurred in the print edition of The Wall Street
elements.label: text

elements.text: Journal in its regular column entitled “Money Rates” plus 3% divided by 365. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: prime rate is not published, then Freddie Mac will determine a comparable rate.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Compensatory fee with respect to principal and interest or payoffs
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to principal and interest or payoffs, Freddie Mac assesses this compensatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee when the Servicer fails to have sufficient funds available for Freddie Mac to draft on
elements.label: text

elements.text: the P&I Draft Date or on the Payoff Draft Date, as applicable. Freddie Mac calculates
elements.label: text

elements.text: this compensatory fee by the amount of the principal and interest, or payoff, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, multiplied by the number of calendar days the draft of principal and interest,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or payoff, was late as a result of the failure of the Servicer to have sufficient funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: available, multiplied by the highest quoted prime rate on the last Business Day of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month in which the draft failure occurred in the print edition of The Wall Street Journal
elements.label: text

elements.text: in its regular column entitled “Money Rates” plus 3% divided by 365. If the prime rate is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not published, then Freddie Mac will determine a comparable rate.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: violations the Servicer commits in any consecutive 12-month period starting with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: month in which the first violation occurred and as shown below:

elements.text: Chapter 8304: Managing Custodial Accounts
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8304.1: Managing Custodial Accounts and Freddie
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mac’s rights (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Managing custodial accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicer responsibilities related to Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Managing custodial accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Custodial Account is a Demand Deposit Account or Interest-Bearing Deposit Account the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must establish and maintain at an Eligible Depository for the safekeeping of funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: associated with Freddie Mac-owned Mortgages. The Servicer must maintain Principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Payments and Escrow Funds in separate Custodial Accounts until Freddie Mac drafts
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly Principal and Interest Payments or loan payoffs or the Servicer remits funds to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: third party. Only funds received in connection with the Servicing of Freddie Mac-owned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages are to be deposited into the accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 8302 for requirements on establishing Custodial Accounts and this
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapter for requirements for administering and reconciling the cash in the Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All Freddie Mac accounts must be reconciled within 45 days of the end of each Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle. The Servicer must identify and fund any shortages within 90 days of the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle even if the variance has not been identified. However, Freddie Mac reserves the right
elements.label: text

elements.text: to request that funding occur immediately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Managing Custodial Accounts completes the cycle of the Servicer’s investor accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities. The Servicer must comply with Freddie Mac’s administrative and
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation requirements for Custodial Accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Sections 8304.1 through 8304.3 address administering Custodial Accounts, including Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s requirements for recordkeeping, investing funds, monitoring depository eligibility and
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions for changing or transferring Custodial Accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8304.4 address reconciling Custodial Accounts, including Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for identifying and correcting differences between the Servicer’s records and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Freddie Mac’s rights
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Draft funds directly from the designated Custodial Accounts at any time. To designate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate Custodial Account and authorize Freddie Mac to draft monthly Principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Payments and payoff proceeds, Servicers must submit a completed, executed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: duly authorized Form 1132A, Authorization for Automatic Transfer of Funds from a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal and Interest Custodial Account Through the Automated Clearing House (ACH),
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with Section 8303.1(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For each Custodial Account, request that the Servicer provide information related to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: designated deposit account, including, without limitation, account balance information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: detailed account activities, bank statements and other information as Freddie Mac may
elements.label: text

elements.text: request in its sole discretion (together, the “Account Information”). At Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: direction, the Servicer will provide the Account Information directly to Freddie Mac or
elements.label: text

elements.text: through an approved third party without cost to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assess compensatory fees and/or seek repayment of losses sustained due to errors,
elements.label: text

elements.text: omission or delays by the Servicer in complying with the requirements of this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Servicer responsibilities related to Custodial Accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are responsible for properly reconciling and maintaining the Custodial Account(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and maintaining accurate records and supporting documentation. Servicers are required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: establish and reconcile Custodial Account(s) for each Seller/Servicer number. Servicers may
elements.label: text

elements.text: not consolidate multiple Seller/Servicer numbers into a single reconciliation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must, at all times, maintain records for each Custodial Account in which
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac has an interest that accurately reflect the following information:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Account designations in accordance with the requirements of Section 8302.1(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Account numbers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The amounts of Principal and Interest Payments and Escrow Funds deposited for each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The dates on which funds were deposited
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Supporting documentation of all deposits to and withdrawals from a Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s vested and ascertainable interest in funds deposited into each Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8304.2: Maintaining Custodial Accounts (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investing custodial account funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintaining the Principal and Interest Custodial Account balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintaining the Escrow Custodial Account balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintaining the Buydown Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monitoring depository eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Investing custodial account funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may not invest funds deposited into any Freddie Mac Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Maintaining the Principal and Interest Custodial Account balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain Freddie Mac funds separately from the Servicer’s general ledger account; a
elements.label: text

elements.text: general ledger account is not a substitute for a Demand Deposit account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deposit all Freddie Mac funds into a Demand or Interest-Bearing Deposit Account in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 8302.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that funds are available for Freddie Mac to draft principal and interest or payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts. If the funds available in a Principal and Interest Custodial Account are less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: the amount due to Freddie Mac on the applicable draft date, the Servicer must advance its
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds so that the amount due to Freddie Mac is available before the applicable draft date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a positive daily balance in the Principal and Interest Custodial Account(s) at all
elements.label: text

elements.text: times
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Perform a consolidated reconciliation if the Servicer has more than one Principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Custodial Account under a specific Seller/Servicer number
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may recover any funds it advanced for the payment of delinquent net yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest from subsequent collections of Principal and Interest Payments.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Maintaining the Escrow Custodial Account balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Establish and maintain at least one Escrow Custodial Account for each Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: number, even if the Servicer does not collect Escrow Funds for Mortgages serviced under
elements.label: text

elements.text: that number
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Maintain the Escrow Funds separately from the Servicer’s general ledger account; a
elements.label: text

elements.text: general ledger account is not a substitute for a Demand Deposit Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Deposit all Freddie Mac funds into a Demand or Interest-Bearing Deposit Account in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 8302.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Ensure that each Borrower’s vested interest is ascertainable at all times
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Ensure that funds are available for remittances before disbursing funds to third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Maintain a positive daily balance in the Escrow Custodial Account(s) at all times
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Perform a consolidated reconciliation if the Servicer has more than one Escrow Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account under a specific Seller/Servicer number
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Deposit an advance from the Servicer’s funds to the Escrow Custodial Account for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortages on the same day the shortage occurs and before making any disbursements. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must not use the overages of one Borrower account to fund the negative Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: or shortage of other Borrower accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may recover any Escrow advance from subsequent payments to the Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account of the specific Mortgage for which the Servicer made the advance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Maintaining the Buydown Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may deposit buydown funds into the Escrow Custodial Account, or the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may, at its option, open a separate Custodial Account for buydown funds. If the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: establishes a separate Buydown Custodial Account, the Servicer must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements regarding Custodial Accounts generally, and the maintenance and
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation of Escrow Custodial Accounts set forth in the Guide for such Buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Monitoring depository eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain all Custodial Accounts in an Eligible Depository at all times. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must have its own oversight process to continually monitor its depository to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: it is viable and in good standing and meets the requirements of Section 8302.2(b). The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s obligation to transfer funds to an Eligible Depository is not dependent upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: notification from Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may determine a depository’s eligibility by subscribing to any of the rating
elements.label: text

elements.text: services referenced in Section 8302.2(c) or by contacting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Institutional_Eligibility@FreddieMac.com. In addition, the Servicer may determine a
elements.label: text

elements.text: depository’s ineligibility based on actual knowledge.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer determines that a depository is no longer eligible, it must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Open new Custodial Accounts within 30 days from the release date of the ratings that
elements.label: text

elements.text: make the depository ineligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Meet the requirements listed in Sections 8304.3(c) and 8304.3(d) for changing or
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferring accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8304.3: Transferring or changing Custodial Accounts
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac's right to remove accounts from specified depositories
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s right to change or transfer accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements for changing or transferring accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Liability for Custodial Account transfer costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Freddie Mac's right to remove accounts from specified depositories
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to determine, at Freddie Mac’s sole discretion, that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may no longer deposit or hold Principal and Interest Payments and Escrow Funds in
elements.label: text

elements.text: a particular depository. Freddie Mac’s exercise of its rights under this section means that it
elements.label: text

elements.text: may expressly prohibit a Servicer from maintaining Custodial Accounts with a particular
elements.label: text

elements.text: depository.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac determines that a depository no longer meets Freddie Mac’s requirements,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will give the Servicer written notice to remove the Custodial Accounts. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must comply with all requirements of the notice and take the following actions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Close the Custodial Accounts within the time frame as specified in the notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Open new Custodial Accounts in accordance with the requirements of Section 8304.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: in an Eligible Depository other than the depository specified in the notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) The Servicer’s right to change or transfer accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may choose to change Custodial Accounts or transfer them to another Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Depository. If the Servicer chooses to do so, it must adhere to the requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Requirements for changing or transferring accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Changes to or transfers of Custodial Accounts may result from any of the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The ineligibility of a depository according to the requirements of Section 8304.2(e)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A written notice from Freddie Mac to remove the account according to the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Section 8304.3(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s choice to change or transfer the account according to the requirements of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8304.3(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A change to the depository’s name, account number or bank routing number
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must meet the requirements of Sections 8302.1(b) through 8302.1(d),
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8302.2(b) and 8302.2(c) to change or transfer any Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Remittance drafting account
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to meeting the requirements above, if there is any change or transfer that
elements.label: text

elements.text: affects the Custodial Account the Servicer chose as a remittance drafting account, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must also:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Contact Freddie Mac (see Directory 1)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Submit the documentation required by Section 8302.1(d) at least 15 Business Days
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Servicer plans to use the new account or modified bank routing instruction
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Continue to use the existing account or bank routing instructions until Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: gives the Servicer written authorization to use the new account or modified bank
elements.label: text

elements.text: routing instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Transfer funds from the former Custodial Account to the new Custodial Account when
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer receives Freddie Mac’s written notice that Freddie Mac has processed the
elements.label: text

elements.text: new remittance instructions
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac drafts and funds are not available because the Servicer changed its
elements.label: text

elements.text: drafting account without receiving Freddie Mac’s written authorization, the Servicer will
elements.label: text

elements.text: be subject to an interest reimbursement compensatory fee as set forth in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Liability for Custodial Account transfer costs
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not be liable to the Servicer for any costs, fees, penalties, loss of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: income or any other expenses directly or indirectly resulting from the transfer of any
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account from one depository to another. Refer to Sections 8304.2(e) through
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8304.3(b). This applies whether Freddie Mac requires the Servicer, or the Servicer chooses,
elements.label: text

elements.text: to transfer the Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8304.4: Reconciling Custodial Accounts (09/10/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling custodial accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling the Principal and Interest Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s Principal and Interest Custodial Account review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling deposits to the Principal and Interest Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling withdrawals from the Principal and Interest Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling the Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac’s Escrow Custodial Account review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling deposits to the Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling withdrawals from the Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reconciling an optional Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Reconciling custodial accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must reconcile all Custodial Accounts within 45 days of the end of each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle. The purpose of the Custodial Account reconciliation is to enable the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify, at the loan level, variances between the Servicer’s receipts, cash disbursements
elements.label: text

elements.text: and liabilities due to third parties and Freddie Mac’s records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify and correct the root cause of the items causing the variance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify Freddie Mac of errors in Freddie Mac’s records
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Fund shortages and clear all variances no later than 90 days from the end of each
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle even if the Servicer has not identified the root cause. However,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to request that funding occur immediately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Reconciling the Principal and Interest Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not consolidate multiple Seller/Servicer numbers into a single
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation. In reconciling the Principal and Interest Custodial Account, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Reconcile the Principal and Interest Custodial Account within 45 days from the end of
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Establish a separate Custodial Account and reconciliation for each Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: number
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Use the ending balance of the Monthly Account Statement that corresponds to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Consolidate all Principal and Interest Custodial Accounts for a specific Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: number into a single reconciliation, if the Servicer has multiple Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Accounts for a specific Seller/Servicer number
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Properly document and account for any prepaid and delinquent interest in order to
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurately determine the Custodial Account adjusted liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Identify, at the loan level, variances between the Servicer’s receipts, cash disbursements
elements.label: text

elements.text: and liabilities due to third parties and Freddie Mac’s records
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Fund shortages and clear all variances within 90 days from the end of the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle, even if the Servicer has not identified the root cause of the issue. However,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to request that funding occur immediately.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Use the Form 59, Principal and Interest Custodial Account Reconciliation - Monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account Statement (MAS), to reconcile the account and identify variances. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may use either the online version of Form 59 or a copy that is an exact replica of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: form, to complete the reconciliation. The Servicer must document every line item on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 59 of the custodial account reconciliation as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bank statements with a running daily balance that cover the Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bank statements that show deposits in transit, outstanding debits and funding of
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortage variances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The final version of the MAS
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquent and prepaid interest trial balances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Supporting documentation for other billings
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cumulative variance logs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any additional documentation requested by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer disagrees with the ending balance or any adjustments listed on the Draft
elements.label: text

elements.text: Report with the exception of short sale charge-offs applied to the MAS (see Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9208.3(a)), the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 7) within 90 days of the end
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Accounting Cycle represented by the MAS. Freddie Mac reserves the right to deny the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s request for a refund or interest on a refund if the Servicer does not notify Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac within the required time frame.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 8304.4(c) for information on Freddie Mac’s review of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Freddie Mac’s Principal and Interest Custodial Account review
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac conducts periodic examinations of the Principal and Interest Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reconciliation. At any time, Freddie Mac may request, and the Servicer must provide,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac with a complete reconciliation no later than the requested due date. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation package must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A completed and fully executed Form 59, Principal and Interest Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reconciliation Worksheet — Monthly Account Statement, along with supporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation for every line item on Form 59
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bank statements, including any that show evidence of deposits of shortage variances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Trial balance of delinquent and prepaid interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Variance logs and any supplemental information to explain the nature and age of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: variance displaying the Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any additional documentation requested by the Freddie Mac examiner
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Reconciling deposits to the Principal and Interest Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: In reconciling a Principal and Interest Custodial Account, the Servicer must, as of the end of
elements.label: text

elements.text: each Accounting Cycle, account for all payments received as well as advances and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: billings due to Freddie Mac. Section 8302.1(f) specifies the time frames the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: meet for the deposit of the funds listed below. The required deposits to the Principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Interest Custodial Account by source are as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Freddie Mac’s share of payments received:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Principal and interest collected on all Freddie Mac Mortgages, whether received
elements.label: text

elements.text: from, or on behalf of, the Borrower or applied from a buydown account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Principal curtailments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payoffs, including short sales and third-party foreclosure sale proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prepayment penalties, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer chooses, it may also deposit and later withdraw the Servicing fees that it
elements.label: text

elements.text: earns. If the Servicer does not withdraw these funds by the end of the Accounting Cycle,
elements.label: text

elements.text: it must account for them in its reconciliation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Advances and other billings due from the Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advances for remittance shortages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest due for newly funded Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Repurchase proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Reconciling withdrawals from the Principal and Interest Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: In reconciling the Principal and Interest Custodial Account, the Servicer must, as of the end
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Accounting Cycle, account for all withdrawals from the account, including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Principal and interest and payoffs to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Earned Service fees and amounts in excess of the Minimum Gross Yield originally
elements.label: text

elements.text: deposited with a full Principal and Interest Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Participant’s share of Principal and Interest Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Recovery of advances for delinquent Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deposit errors and other reconciling items
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers to a Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account. Refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8304.4(j) for reconciliation requirements regarding this optional Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Reconciling the Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not consolidate multiple Seller/Servicer numbers into a single
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation. In reconciling the Escrow Custodial Account, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Reconcile the Escrow Custodial Account within 45 days from the end of the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Establish a separate bank account and reconciliation for each Seller/Servicer number
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Complete a consolidated reconciliation if the Servicer has more than one Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account under a specific Seller/Servicer number, and a consolidated
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation for its Escrow liabilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Use the positive Escrow balance that corresponds to the end of the Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Properly document and account for all Escrow balances in order to accurately determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Escrow Custodial Account adjusted liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Identify, at the loan level, variances between the Servicer’s receipts, cash disbursements
elements.label: text

elements.text: and liabilities due to third parties and Freddie Mac’s records
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Fund shortages and clear all variances within 90 days from the end of the Accounting
elements.label: text

elements.text: Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Include any buydown balances in the Servicer's Escrow Custodial Account reconciliation,
elements.label: text

elements.text: if any buydown funds are deposited into the Escrow Custodial Account. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may complete a separate reconciliation for buydown subsidies if the Servicer chooses to
elements.label: text

elements.text: set up an optional buydown Custodial Account. Refer to Section 8304.4(j) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for reconciling a separate buydown Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9. Reconcile to the positive Escrow balance as of the end of the Accounting Cycle using
elements.label: text

elements.text: Form 59E, Escrow Custodial Account Reconciliation Worksheet. The Servicer may use
elements.label: text

elements.text: either the online version of Form 59E or a copy that is an exact replica of the form, to
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete the reconciliation. The Servicer must document every line item on Form 59E of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Escrow Custodial Account reconciliation as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bank statement(s) with a running daily balance that cover the Accounting Cycle
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bank statements that show deposits in transit, outstanding debits, and funding of
elements.label: text

elements.text: shortage variances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan level trial balance displaying the Escrow balances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If the Servicer’s trial balance nets the escrow liability, and add the negative escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: balance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cumulative variance logs that display the Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 8304.4(g) for information on Freddie Mac’s review of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Freddie Mac’s Escrow Custodial Account review
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac conducts periodic examinations of the Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reconciliation. At any time, Freddie Mac may request, and the Servicer must provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac with, a complete reconciliation no later than the requested due date. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciliation package must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A completed and fully executed Form 59E, Escrow Custodial Account Reconciliation
elements.label: text

elements.text: Worksheet, along with supporting documentation for every line item on Form 59E
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Bank statements, including any that show evidence of deposits of shortage variances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Trial balance displaying all Escrow liabilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Variance logs and any supplemental information to explain the nature and age of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: variance displaying the Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any additional documentation requested by the Freddie Mac examiner
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer’s trial balance reflects a net Escrow balance, the Servicer must add the
elements.label: text

elements.text: negative Escrow to determine the liability.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may use either the online version of Form 59E, or a copy that is an exact
elements.label: text

elements.text: replication of the form, to complete the reconciliation. Upon Freddie Mac’s request, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must be able to provide Freddie Mac with a paper copy of the completed Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 59E, together with any supporting documentation, for up to one year from the date of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: related reconciliation worksheet. All documentation must be received by Freddie Mac no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than the requested Due Date. Refer to the instructions section of Form 59E for
elements.label: text

elements.text: information on supporting documentation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (h) Reconciling deposits to the Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: In reconciling the Escrow Custodial Account, the Servicer must, as of the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle, account for all Escrow Funds received, as well as advances or interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: paid on Escrow. The Servicer is required to maintain an active Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: even if no Escrow amounts are collected from the Borrower(s). The Escrow Custodial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Account may also be used for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deposit partial payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Suspense amounts where the Borrower has not established clear intent for the payment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insurance payment proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Section 8302.1(f) specifies the time frames the Servicer must meet for the deposit of
elements.label: text

elements.text: funds to Custodial Accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following details the deposits to include in the reconciliation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Payments received:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Escrow Funds paid by the Borrower or deposited on the Borrower’s behalf, such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance loss claim proceeds
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Payments held in unapplied or suspense pending proper distribution such as, partial
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments and biweekly payments from plans that allow for biweekly collection of
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments on monthly amortizing Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Buydown funds, if applicable. Refer to Section 8304.4(j) for reconciliation
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements regarding an optional buydown Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Funds due from the Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advances as specified in Section 8304.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest on Escrow, if paid and deposited to the Borrower’s Escrow account
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reconciling withdrawals from the Escrow Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: In reconciling the Escrow Custodial Account, the Servicer must, as of the end of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Accounting Cycle, account for all withdrawals made from the account. The following lists
elements.label: text

elements.text: the withdrawals to include in the reconciliation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Disbursements of Escrow Funds, including insurance proceeds resulting from a loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Partial payments applied, including biweekly payment plan funds on monthly amortizing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Buydown funds applied to the Borrower’s Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Recovery of the Servicer’s advances when the Servicer receives a subsequent Escrow
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment for the Mortgage for which the Servicer made the advance
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Refunds of surplus Escrow Funds resulting from an Escrow analysis or Mortgage payoff
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Deposit errors and other reconciling items
elements.label: text

elements.text: (j) Reconciling an optional Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer chooses to open an optional Custodial Account, as referenced in Sections
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8302.1(a) and 8302.2(a), as of the end of the Accounting Cycle, the Servicer must complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following consolidated reconciliations:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Principal and Interest Disbursement Clearing Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The balance of this type of Custodial Account must equal zero or the amount of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Principal and Interest Payment or payoff amount due to Freddie Mac. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: only make deposits to this type of Custodial Account immediately prior to the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: draft date. The Servicer must consolidate the Principal and Interest Disbursement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Clearing Custodial Account reconciliation with the Servicer’s Principal and Interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Custodial Account reconciliation. Refer to Sections 8304.4(a) through 8304.4(e) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements on reconciling the Principal and Interest Custodial Account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Buydown Custodial Account
elements.label: text

elements.text: The balance of this type of Custodial Account must equal the total of buydown funds
elements.label: text

elements.text: deposited at the time Freddie Mac purchased a Mortgage, minus the monthly buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount times the number of payments applied since Freddie Mac’s purchase. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: balance in this Custodial Account may represent the buydown balance of more than one
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage. Refer to Section 8304.4(a) for requirements on reconciling this account.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8401: Changes to the Mortgaged
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8401.1: Release, easement and condominium
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: conversion requirements (07/09/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Partial releases, easements and acquisitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibition of conversion of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Condemnation and eminent domain
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Modification, waiver or release of terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Partial releases, easements and acquisitions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Processing requests for partial releases and granting of easements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer may receive a request to release part of the Mortgaged Premises as security
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the Mortgage (a partial release) or a request to approve the granting of an easement on
elements.label: text

elements.text: a portion of the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A partial release may be initiated at the Borrower’s request or as a result of some other
elements.label: text

elements.text: action such as a condemnation or a taking of the property by eminent domain. Except as
elements.label: text

elements.text: noted below, the Servicer must obtain an appraisal report with an interior and exterior
elements.label: text

elements.text: inspection that meets the requirements of Topic 5600 when considering such requests in
elements.label: text

elements.text: order to determine the value of the Mortgaged Premises immediately before the release
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the estimated value after the release.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer, under its delegated authority, may approve a partial release or grant an
elements.label: text

elements.text: easement provided the following conditions are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s monthly Mortgage payment is current at the time of the request and
elements.label: text

elements.text: there is no change in the expectation that the Borrower can continue to make the
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has received written approval of the FHA, VA, RHS or MI and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: superior lienholders, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least 12 months have passed since the Origination Date, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If either of the situations and the corresponding actions in the table below are met:
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding the above, the Servicer may require that any consideration received by

elements.text: the Servicer determines that the intended use of the released land or easement would

elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon release, the Servicer must ensure that all applicable tax authorities are notified for
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: required in Section 4201.2 and the title insurance meets the requirements of Chapter 4702
