elements.text: after the partial release or easement has been granted.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If any of the above conditions are not met, the Servicer must complete and submit Form
elements.label: text

elements.text: 715, Borrower Application for Partial Release or Easement, to Freddie Mac via e-mail at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Distressed_Property@FreddieMac.com or fax at (571) 382-4933.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Additional requirements for easements
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements noted in Section 8401.1(a)(i), the Servicer must ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: that any special requirements for the granting of an easement as specified in the Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument are met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Land acquisition
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not object to the acquisition of land if the land to be added to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is free and clear of title and does not pose any risks that could
elements.label: text

elements.text: devalue Freddie Mac’s asset.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Prohibition of conversion of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgaged Premises may not be converted from a 1- to 4-unit property to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Condominium Unit or Cooperative Unit, and any such conversion shall be treated as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer of the Mortgaged Premises under Section 8406.1(a). In addition, no individual units
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a 2- to 4-unit property may be separately released from the Mortgaged Premises.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Condemnation and eminent domain
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the requirements noted in Section 8401.1(a), the following conditions must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: met in the case of a condemnation or a taking of the property by eminent domain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must ensure that any special requirements for a condemnation or the taking
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a property by eminent domain as specified in the Security Instrument are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer must submit the documentation specified in Section 8401.1(a) and any other
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable documentation to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgaged Premises will be taken in whole and the consideration to be paid to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower will be insufficient to satisfy the UPB of the Mortgage, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgaged Premises will be taken in part and:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The value of the property being taken is greater than the consideration being paid
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Borrower; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The LTV ratio does not meet the conditions specified in Section 8401.1(a) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: release without any consideration
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will review the request and provide the Servicer with instructions on how to
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Modification, waiver or release of terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not modify, waive or release any term of any Note or Security Instrument,
elements.label: text

elements.text: accept any prepayment or consent to any postponement of performance by any Borrower of
elements.label: text

elements.text: any obligation under a Note or Security Instrument, except as authorized by the Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For leasehold Mortgages, the Servicer must not approve or consent to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any partition, subdivision or modification of the ground lease community and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: leasehold estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any surrender, abandonment or termination of the leasehold estate or the ground lease
elements.label: text

elements.text: community
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The termination or cancelation of the lease and any amendments to the lease that affect
elements.label: text

elements.text: the rights of the leasehold mortgagee, without the written approval of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8402: Property Seizure
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8402.1: Property seizure and disclosure of pre-seizure
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: information (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property seizure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unsolicited disclosure of pre-seizure information and property requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requested disclosure of pre-seizure reporting and property requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reimbursement of expenses related to property seizures
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Property seizure
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under various federal, State and local laws, a law enforcement agency may, in some
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, seize and cause the forfeiture of real property that is obtained with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceeds of activities, or which is used to facilitate activities, committed in violation of
elements.label: text

elements.text: federal, State or local laws. Such laws include those relating to controlled substances,
elements.label: text

elements.text: gambling and prostitution. Actions that result in the loss of possession of real property by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: legal action or process are called property seizures. These different laws commonly provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: some avenue of relief from forfeiture for a mortgagee that can prove it is an “innocent
elements.label: text

elements.text: owner” (or “innocent lienholder”).
elements.label: text

elements.text: An important course of action in protecting the mortgagee’s innocent owner status is prompt
elements.label: text

elements.text: and cooperative communication with law enforcement agencies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) The Servicer’s designee for contacts with law enforcement agencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: To properly handle the sensitive area of mortgagee-law enforcement agency cooperation
elements.label: text

elements.text: and maximize mutual benefits from such cooperation, the Servicer must limit contacts
elements.label: text

elements.text: with or from law enforcement agencies to a designated management-level employee that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer selects. The Servicer must alert its employees to direct all information
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding a property seizure to the appropriate designee, including, but not limited to, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unsolicited information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Government notices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Government or other third-party inquiries
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Telephone calls or written correspondence
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The management-level employee selected as the Servicer’s designee to handle a property
elements.label: text

elements.text: seizure must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain complete, detailed and accurate records of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All information received, relayed or reported, including (where applicable) the
elements.label: text

elements.text: names of all contacting and contacted parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All actions taken by the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The dates of all contacts and actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confirm in writing communications with Freddie Mac and appropriate law
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforcement agencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deliver the original Mortgage file to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) when and if
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac requests the Servicer to do so
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Restriction on foreclosure action
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must contact Freddie Mac to request approval to initiate foreclosure (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Applicability of requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The requirements in Sections 8402.1(a) and 8402.1(d) address forfeitures arising from
elements.label: text

elements.text: violations of federal, State or local laws specified in Section 8402.1(a)(i), above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, all of these requirements also extend to government seizure and forfeiture of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property for the Borrower’s violation of any other laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9401.1(b) for Freddie Mac rights when the requirements of this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section and Sections 8402.1(b) through 8402.2(b) regarding government seizure and
elements.label: text

elements.text: forfeiture of the property are not met.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Unsolicited disclosure of pre-seizure information and property requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must relay the following information to the asset forfeiture team in the Drug
elements.label: text

elements.text: Enforcement Administration (DEA) office nearest to the location of the property:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Unsolicited information that the Servicer receives from any party (other than a law
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforcement official) regarding a possible connection between alleged or suspected drug
elements.label: text

elements.text: transactions and the Mortgage or the property. The Servicer must relay the information
elements.label: text

elements.text: without verifying or passing judgment on its source or veracity.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Visual observations of suspected drug transactions by any party on the property as
elements.label: text

elements.text: reported by a Servicer’s employee or a contractor while on a routine inspection of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must cooperate with all law enforcement agencies to the extent that applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: law allows. If the DEA or another law enforcement agency requests information about the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, Mortgage or property or requests that the Servicer take certain actions, whether or
elements.label: text

elements.text: not in response to a voluntary disclosure of information, the Servicer must determine if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The request complies with the provisions of applicable law, including, but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Right to Financial Privacy Act (RFPA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s compliance with the request is allowed under applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s compliance with the request would expose the Servicer or the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: staff to potential harm and/or liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within three Business Days of receiving the DEA’s or other law enforcement agency’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: request, the Servicer must report the information in writing to the DEA or other law
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforcement agency. The Servicer must send a copy of that report to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 5) within three Business Days of reporting the information to the DEA or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: law enforcement agency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When submitting information to the DEA, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advise the DEA that the Servicer is Servicing the Mortgage for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disclose to the DEA the name, telephone number and mailing address of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s contact at Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Forward the information to any other applicable federal or local law enforcement
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency as the DEA may request
elements.label: text

elements.text: All reporting to the DEA and/or to Freddie Mac must be in writing unless an emergency
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrants reporting by telephone, in which case the Servicer must confirm such report in
elements.label: text

elements.text: writing to Freddie Mac in accordance with Section 8402.1(a)(ii). The Servicer must also
elements.label: text

elements.text: report all developments after the initial contact to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) within
elements.label: text

elements.text: three Business Days of any such development.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must order a property inspection within three Business Days of receiving
elements.label: text

elements.text: unsolicited information regarding a possible connection or observation of alleged or
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspected drug transactions in connection with the property. Freddie Mac will permit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: curbside inspection as specified in Section 9202.3(c). The Servicer must submit a copy of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the inspection report along with the Freddie Mac loan number and the name and phone
elements.label: text

elements.text: number of the Servicer’s point of contact to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) within three
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Days of the Servicer’s receipt of the report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Requested disclosure of pre-seizure information and property requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must cooperate with all law enforcement agencies to the extent that applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: law allows. If the DEA or another law enforcement agency requests information about the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower, the Mortgage or the property or requests that the Servicer take certain actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (whether or not in response to a voluntary unsolicited disclosure of information by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer as provided in Section 8402.1(a)), the Servicer must determine if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The request complies with the provisions of applicable law, including, but not limited to,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the RFPA
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s compliance with the request is allowed under applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s compliance with the request would expose the Servicer or the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: staff to potential harm and/or liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within three Business Days of receiving the DEA’s or other law enforcement agency’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: request, the Servicer must report the request to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) with a
elements.label: text

elements.text: copy of the Servicer’s response to the agency and any other information the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: submitted. The Servicer must also report to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) all
elements.label: text

elements.text: developments after the initial contact within five Business Days of any such
elements.label: text

elements.text: development.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When submitting information to the DEA or another law enforcement agency, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Advise the agency that the Servicer is Servicing the Mortgage for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disclose to the agency the name, telephone number and mailing address of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s contact at Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Forward the information to any other applicable federal or local law enforcement
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency as may be requested by the law enforcement agency making the request
elements.label: text

elements.text: All reporting to the DEA and/or to Freddie Mac must be in writing unless an emergency
elements.label: text

elements.text: warrants reporting by telephone, in which case the Servicer must confirm such report in
elements.label: text

elements.text: writing to Freddie Mac in accordance with Section 8402.1(a). The Servicer must also
elements.label: text

elements.text: report all developments after the initial contact to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) within
elements.label: text

elements.text: three Business Days of any such development.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must order a property inspection within three Business Days of receiving a
elements.label: text

elements.text: request from a law enforcement agency. Freddie Mac will permit a curbside inspection as
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in Section 9202.3(c). The Servicer must submit a copy of the inspection report
elements.label: text

elements.text: along with the Freddie Mac loan number and the name and phone number of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s point of contact to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) within three Business Days
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Servicer’s receipt of the report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Reimbursement of expenses related to property seizures
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will reimburse the Servicer for allowable expenses listed in Exhibit 57A,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Approved Attorney, Foreclosure, Mediation, Postponement Fees and Title Expenses, that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer incurs to comply with Freddie Mac’s requirements regarding property seizure only
elements.label: text

elements.text: if the expenses are not legally collectible from the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac’s written pre-approval prior to incurring expenses that
elements.label: text

elements.text: are not listed, or that exceed Freddie Mac’s expense limits in Exhibit 57A by submitting a
elements.label: text

elements.text: request for pre-approval via PAID (Payments Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools). If unusual or emergency circumstances do not allow the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer to request Freddie Mac’s prior written approval, then the Servicer must notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac via PAID by the next Business Day after the Servicer incurred the expense. If
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer’s determination to incur the expense was reasonable, Freddie Mac will
elements.label: text

elements.text: reimburse the Servicer for the expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8402.2: Government notice of property seizure to the
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Servicer (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Government notice of property seizure to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Government notice of property seizure to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Government notice of property seizure to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Servicer receives notice that a law enforcement agency has seized and/or intends to
elements.label: text

elements.text: initiate forfeiture proceedings against the property, the Servicer must comply with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures detailed below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reporting to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within two Business Days of receiving the notice of seizure and/or intent to initiate
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure proceedings, the Servicer must forward such notice to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 5). With the notice, the Servicer must submit all of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The following information related to the Mortgage:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Freddie Mac loan number
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Mortgage status (current, delinquent or in foreclosure)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ UPB, interest and per diem interest amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any other outstanding amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any other information regarding the Borrower or the property that the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: deems relevant to the law enforcement agency’s action
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the Note and any modifying instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the Security Instrument with applicable recording information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of all assignments of the Security Instrument with applicable recording
elements.label: text

elements.text: information relating to the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Such other documents and information as Freddie Mac may request
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon receiving the required documents from the Servicer, Freddie Mac will forward such
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents to Freddie Mac’s Legal Division, which will assume responsibility for
elements.label: text

elements.text: representing Freddie Mac and the Servicer, where applicable, in the forfeiture
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceedings. Where warranted, the Servicer will receive appropriate instructions from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgage is delinquent, the Servicer must report to Freddie Mac via an EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmission within the first three Business Days of the month following the month that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the law enforcement agency seized the property unless the Mortgage was current. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage is current and a law enforcement agency seized the property, the Servicer may,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but is not required to, report the event in the EDR transmission. Use default action code
elements.label: text

elements.text: 24 (Government seizure) and provide the date that the seizure occurred.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: For additional information on EDR requirements, refer to Section 9102.6.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reporting to the FHA, RHS, VA or MI
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report the seizure and Freddie Mac’s role in seeking relief from
elements.label: text

elements.text: forfeiture to the FHA, RHA, VA or MI, as applicable, and relay any response to Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac (see Directory 5) within three Business Days of receiving the response. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must include a copy of any instructions issued by the FHA, RHA, VA or MI as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: result of the Servicer’s report and, unless otherwise instructed by Freddie Mac, must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with such instructions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Property inspection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must order a property inspection on the property within three Business Days
elements.label: text

elements.text: of receiving a notice of property seizure from a law enforcement agency. Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will permit a curbside inspection as specified in Section 9202.3(c)(ii). The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: submit a copy of the inspection report along with the Freddie Mac loan number and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: name and phone number of the Servicer’s point of contact to Freddie Mac (see Directory
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5) within three Business Days of the Servicer’s receipt of the report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Government notice of property seizure to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Freddie Mac receives a notice of seizure and/or intent to forfeit, it will notify the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: as soon as possible. Within three Business Days of Freddie Mac’s notification to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer, or sooner if requested, the Servicer must submit to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5)
elements.label: text

elements.text: all of the documents and information cited in Section 8402.2(a) and comply with all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of that section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8403: Abandoned Properties, Distressed
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Properties and Properties that Pose a Risk of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8403.1: Servicing Mortgages on Abandoned
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Properties, distressed properties and properties that
elements.label: text

elements.text: pose a Risk of Property Ownership (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Mortgages on distressed properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Properties that pose a Risk of Property Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing Mortgages on Abandoned Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are responsible for acting without delay, and in an efficient and responsible manner to
elements.label: text

elements.text: protect both the Servicer’s and Freddie Mac’s interests when the Servicer becomes aware of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises that becomes a distressed property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9401.1(b) for information regarding Freddie Mac’s rights when the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer becomes aware of a distressed property and fails to act to protect both the Servicer’s and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s interests as required by this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicing Mortgages on distressed properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: A distressed property is real property subject to a Mortgage that may or may not pose a Risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Property Ownership (see Section 9202.2(b)) to Freddie Mac and is a property that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requires substantial repairs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has sustained significant physical deterioration; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has been condemned by a local authority
elements.label: text

elements.text: Even if the owner has abandoned the Mortgaged Premises, a Servicer must report all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages on distressed properties that are 30 or more days delinquent via an EDR
elements.label: text

elements.text: transmission within the first three Business Days of the month following the month in which
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer identified the problem using default reason code 011 (Property problem) and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable occupancy code.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a property is distressed, the Servicer must conduct a search of the records of the local code
elements.label: text

elements.text: authority to determine if there is any outstanding health or safety violations filed against
elements.label: text

elements.text: either the Borrower or the property. If a Servicer discovers any code violations on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: distressed property, the Servicer must report them to Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) within
elements.label: text

elements.text: three Business Days of identifying the violation. Servicers must attach a copy of the code
elements.label: text

elements.text: violation and copies of the most recent six consecutive months (or less, depending on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: level of Delinquency) of Form 1013, 1-4 Unit Property Inspection Report.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to any reporting requirement set forth above for distressed properties, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a record of when the condition was discovered and take all necessary actions to
elements.label: text

elements.text: protect the property from waste, damage and vandalism and prevent any loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Inspect the property monthly until the condition is resolved
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that property insurance coverage is maintained. This includes taking all actions
elements.label: text

elements.text: required in the mortgage clause of all applicable property insurance policies, including,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but not limited to, providing all notices to the insurer required under such clause, in order
elements.label: text

elements.text: to preserve the coverage and its maximum benefits for the Servicer and/or Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as mortgagee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ File a claim with the applicable property insurer on Freddie Mac’s behalf if the property
elements.label: text

elements.text: is damaged and the Borrower has not filed a claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Comply with the requirements of the VA, RHS, FHA or MI, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must obtain Freddie Mac’s written pre-approval before incurring expenses on
elements.label: text

elements.text: distressed properties that exceed the limits contained in Exhibit 57, 1- to 4-Unit Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Approved Expense Amounts, by submitting a request for pre-approval via PAID (Payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Automated Intelligent and Dynamic) (see Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If unusual or emergency circumstances do not allow a Servicer to request Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior written approval, then the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via PAID by the next
elements.label: text

elements.text: Business Day after the Servicer incurred the expense. If the Servicer’s determination to incur
elements.label: text

elements.text: the expense was reasonable, as determined in Freddie Mac’s sole discretion, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will reimburse the expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval to initiate foreclosure of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage on a distressed property as required in Section 9301.2(e).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Properties that pose a Risk of Property Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a property securing a Mortgage has been identified as posing a Risk of Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership, the Servicer must contact Freddie Mac and report the Mortgage to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Directory 5), even if the Mortgage is not delinquent, within three Business Days of
elements.label: text

elements.text: identifying the risk. In such instances, the Servicer must attach copies of the most recent six
elements.label: text

elements.text: consecutive months (or less, depending on the level of Delinquency) of Form 1013 and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other relevant information when reporting the Risk of Property Ownership to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Servicing Mortgages on Abandoned Properties
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer is responsible for acting without delay and in an efficient and responsible manner
elements.label: text

elements.text: to protect both the Servicer’s and Freddie Mac’s interests when the Servicer becomes aware
elements.label: text

elements.text: of an Abandoned Property.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9401.1(b) for information regarding Freddie Mac’s rights when
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer becomes aware of a distressed property and fails to act to protect both Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Freddie Mac’s interests as required by this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Defined term
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following additional terms are used in this chapter:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Abandoned Property definition
elements.label: text

elements.text: A
elements.label: text

elements.text: Abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Abandoned Property is real property to which the owner has
elements.label: text

elements.text: voluntarily and intentionally relinquished possession, claim and
elements.label: text

elements.text: control, or real property defined as abandoned property by applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: laws. Conditions that may lead to abandonment include vacancy,
elements.label: text

elements.text: waste, deterioration, lack of utilities or Delinquency.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must report all Mortgages on Abandoned Properties that are 30 or more days
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent to Freddie Mac via an EDR transmission within the first three Business Days
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the month following the month the Servicer determined the property is abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: using occupancy code 07 (Abandoned).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Determining if property is abandoned
elements.label: text

elements.text: In accordance with applicable law, the Servicer must first determine if the Borrower has,
elements.label: text

elements.text: in fact, abandoned the property and then take the following actions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Attempt to locate the Borrower and determine the reason for abandonment
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Protect the property from waste, damage and vandalism, and ensure the continuation
elements.label: text

elements.text: of utilities, where necessary. Exhibit 57 describes the allowable preservation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintenance expenses that may be incurred without obtaining Freddie Mac’s pre￾approval. (Refer to Section 9701.2(d) regarding reimbursement for property
elements.label: text

elements.text: preservation expenses.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Maintain accurate reports of any of these conditions, detailing actions to prevent
elements.label: text

elements.text: losses
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Ensure that property insurance is maintained. This includes taking all actions required
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the mortgage clause of all applicable property insurance policies, including, but not
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to, providing all notices to the insurer required under such clause, in order to
elements.label: text

elements.text: preserve the coverage and its maximum benefits for the Servicer and/or Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as mortgagee. If the property is damaged, file a claim with the applicable property
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance company.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Obtain interior and exterior inspections in accordance with Section 9202.3(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Comply with the requirements of the VA, FHA, RHS or MI, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Initiate foreclosure proceedings if the Mortgage payments are delinquent. (Refer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9301.6(d) regarding preserving the property during the foreclosure process.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Provide to the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) and the Borrower IRS Form 1099-A,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Acquisition or Abandonment of Secured Property, as required under Section 6050J of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Internal Revenue Code. (For IRS filing requirements see Section 8106.2(b).)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval via PAID before incurring
elements.label: text

elements.text: expenses that either exceed the approval limits contained in Exhibit 57 or will be incurred
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the date of a foreclosure sale, including where the property is sold to a third party.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a delay in taking protective action may result in impairment of the property, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must contact Freddie Mac via PAID for immediate approval. If the circumstance
elements.label: text

elements.text: of incurring the expense is such that the Servicer cannot obtain Freddie Mac’s prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval, then the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac via PAID by the next Business Day
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Servicer incurred the expense. If the Servicer’s determination to incur the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expense was reasonable, as determined in Freddie Mac’s sole discretion, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: will reimburse the Servicer for the expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8404: Servicing Mortgages Impacted by
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: a Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8404.1: Servicing Mortgages impacted by a disaster
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Refer to Bulletin 2026-2, which announced updates related to Resolve® reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for repayment plans and forbearance agreements. Servicers may implement
elements.label: text

elements.text: the new requirements prior to the mandatory October 1, 2026 version of this section if they
elements.label: text

elements.text: are operationally ready to do so.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview of Servicer responsibilities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property protection activities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insurance loss settlements after an Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquency management activities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disaster reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transition following disaster-related forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview of Servicer responsibilities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter provides Servicers with our requirements for assisting Borrowers whose
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises or places of employment are located in an Eligible Disaster Area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A disaster may be a natural disaster (e.g., an earthquake, flood or hurricane) or a man-made
elements.label: text

elements.text: disaster (e.g., a civil disturbance) that adversely affects a geographical area encompassing
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties that secure Freddie Mac Mortgages or Borrowers’ places of employment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Property protection activities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: In conjunction with the requirements outlined in Section 8404.1(d), the Servicer is required
elements.label: text

elements.text: to protect the Mortgaged Premises by following the requirements outlined in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of a disaster impacting Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac, a Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ascertain the number of such Mortgages and the extent of the damage that may have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been caused by the disaster for each Mortgaged Premises, which may be completed by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Determining the status of the property through discussions with the Borrower; and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Completing a property inspection per requirements outlined in Section 9202.3(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are reminded that property inspections will be reimbursed based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: limits set forth in Section 9701.2(d). Property inspections that are completed outside
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the requirements set forth in Section 9701.2(d) may not receive reimbursement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secure an Abandoned Property (as defined in Section 8403.1) if the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: has not sustained significant or total damage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide assistance to the Borrower regarding options for local, State or federal disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monitor and coordinate the insurance claim process and the progress of repairs in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 8202.5
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In addition to the requirements of Section 8202.5, comply with the insurance claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: process requirements in accordance with Section 8404.1(c) for Eligible Disasters
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer determines that the disaster has affected a Mortgaged Premises and it may pose
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Risk of Property Ownership (see Section 9202.2(b)), the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: within five Business Days of learning of the situation. In such instances, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: attach copies of the most recent six consecutive months (or less, depending on the level of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency) of Form 1013, 1-4 Unit Property Inspection Report, and any other relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: information when reporting the Risk of Property Ownership to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 8403.1(a) and 8403.1(b) for additional requirements on properties that
elements.label: text

elements.text: become distressed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Insurance loss settlements after an Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgaged Premises has been damaged and is located in an Eligible Disaster Area,
elements.label: text

elements.text: property insurance proceeds for structural losses must be disbursed in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance loss settlements requirements in Section 8202.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Delinquency management activities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event a disaster strikes, it is imperative that Servicers be considerate of the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances and work to obtain quality right party contact (QRPC) with the Borrower as
elements.label: text

elements.text: soon as possible. This section will outline specific activities that a Servicer must take to
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriately manage the Borrower’s Delinquency if the Servicer believes the disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: resulted in a hardship for the Borrower and the disaster is an Eligible Disaster.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Circumstances where QRPC has not been achieved
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer has not yet achieved QRPC, then the Servicer, at its discretion, may place
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Borrower who becomes 31 or more days delinquent due to an Eligible Disaster in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan for up to 90 days, in accordance with the requirements in this section,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the transition following disaster-related forbearance requirements in Section 8404.1(f)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the forbearance-related requirements in Sections 9203.3(a) through 9203.3(f).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower is 31 days or more delinquent at the time of impact due to an Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster, the Servicer must achieve QRPC before approving forbearance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The forbearance plan must not exceed 90 days without either achieving QRPC or
elements.label: text

elements.text: receiving approval from Freddie Mac (see Directory 5). If the Servicer has relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: information regarding the Borrower’s current financial circumstances and/or the extent of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property damage caused by the Eligible Disaster, the Servicer should consider these
elements.label: text

elements.text: factors in its evaluation decision whether to provide forbearance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If QRPC has not been achieved by the end of the forbearance period, then the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must comply with the requirements of Section 8404.1(f)(ii), which provide the transition
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements when QRPC has not been achieved.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Circumstances where QRPC has been achieved
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower does not require any form of relief and there is no Risk of Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership (see Section 9202.2(b)) to Freddie Mac as a result of damage caused by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: disaster, Servicers should continue to service the Mortgage in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower requires a relief option or the Mortgaged Premises has damage that
elements.label: text

elements.text: would pose a Risk of Property Ownership to Freddie Mac, Servicers have discretion to
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspend collection and foreclosure proceedings by placing the Borrower into a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan, based on the circumstances of each case. The Servicer is not required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain a complete Borrower Response Package from the Borrower and may offer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Forbearance for a period of one to three months, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If necessary, one or more successive forbearance plan periods of one to three months
elements.label: text

elements.text: and which collectively may not exceed 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: Without prior approval from Freddie Mac, the forbearance plan may not be extended
elements.label: text

elements.text: beyond a date that would cause the Delinquency to exceed a cumulative total of 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months of the Borrower’s contractual monthly Mortgage payment, including taxes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance if the Servicer is collecting Escrow for those expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When applicable, such a suspension of Servicing action must be pre-approved by the MI,
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA, RHS or VA to avoid jeopardizing benefits of any applicable insurance or guaranty.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should reassess each impacted Borrower on a regular basis during the
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance period to determine if forbearance should be extended up to a total of 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months or the hardship has been resolved.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer believes forbearance beyond a total of 12 months or beyond a total that
elements.label: text

elements.text: would cause the Borrower to exceed 12 months of delinquency is warranted, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must make that recommendation to Freddie Mac for consideration by submitting a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Forbearance Extension Request in Resolve®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event Freddie Mac participated in evaluating a Borrower for forbearance beyond a
elements.label: text

elements.text: total of 12 months and Freddie Mac denied the request, the Servicer must refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1301.2(i) for more information on adverse action notices that must be provided to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower on behalf of Freddie Mac under certain limited circumstances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At the end of the forbearance period, the Servicer must reassess the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances to determine whether the Borrower’s hardship resulting from the Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster has been resolved. The determination may be based on information from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower as a result of QRPC and/or:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An updated property inspection assessment of the extent of property damage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Discussions with the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the hardship has been resolved and the Borrower is ready to transition to a permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: solution, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for the most appropriate workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: option to cure the Delinquency in accordance with the requirements in Section 8404.1(f).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the hardship has not been resolved, then the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility for extended forbearance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Mortgage eligibility and forbearance plan requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, when offering forbearance relief to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster, Servicers must use the requirements set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Chapter 9203.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage may be secured by the following property types:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Second home, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The property may be vacant or condemned but not abandoned.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In situations where the Borrower is unable to send or receive documentation, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may waive the requirement that the forbearance plan be in writing. In these
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, the Servicer may enter the Borrower into a forbearance plan through a
elements.label: text

elements.text: verbal agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Other Delinquency management activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not assess late charges as long as the Borrower is on a forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan or paying as agreed on a repayment plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Disaster reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Credit reporting for Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must report Borrowers who are on a disaster-related forbearance plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: repayment plan or Trial Period Plan to the credit bureaus in accordance with applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: law, including the Fair Credit Reporting Act.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reporting loans affected by a disaster to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must report all Mortgages that are affected by a disaster and are 31 or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: days delinquent to Freddie Mac via an EDR transmission within the first three Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days of the month following the month the Servicer learned of the disaster using default
elements.label: text

elements.text: reason code 034 (Eligible Disaster Area).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Reporting forbearance or repayment plans to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must report all Mortgages that are subject to a repayment plan or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan resulting from disaster-caused hardship via an EDR transmission using
elements.label: text

elements.text: default code 09 for forbearance or default code 12 for a repayment plan within the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: three Business Days of the month following the month that the plan was entered into. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must continue to report that the Mortgage is under the plan until the Mortgage is
elements.label: text

elements.text: fully reinstated or the plan ends.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 9203 for more details.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Transition following disaster-related forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was placed on forbearance as a result of an Eligible Disaster, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must contact the Borrower on a periodic basis and prior to the end of the forbearance period
elements.label: text

elements.text: to determine whether the hardship has been resolved and the most appropriate relief or
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout option to cure the Delinquency. The Servicer must consider a number of factors,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s ability to achieve QRPC
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s current financial circumstances and ability to resume making monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The status of the Mortgage at the time of the disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, at the end of the disaster-related forbearance period, the Servicer is evaluating the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower for a foreclosure prevention alternative based on a Borrower Response Package,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the documentation cannot be more than 180 days old as of the date of the evaluation for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure prevention alternative.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Evaluation hierarchy
elements.label: text

elements.text: If QRPC is established with a Borrower who was current or less than 60 days delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., less than two months delinquent) as of the date the Eligible Disaster occurred, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower is unable to resolve the Delinquency through a reinstatement or repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for the loss mitigation options in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following Disaster evaluation hierarchy:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Freddie Mac Flex Modification®
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Standard Short Sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was not current or less than 60 days delinquent (i.e., less than two months
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent) as of the date of the Eligible Disaster, the Servicer must conduct all loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigation evaluations in accordance with the standard loss mitigation evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: hierarchy, as described in Section 9201.2, or must submit an exception request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In most cases, Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster who qualify to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluated for a Disaster Payment Deferral will be transitioning from a forbearance plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but forbearance is not a prerequisite.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Solicitation for a Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must proactively solicit the Borrower for a Disaster Payment Deferral in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Section 9203.4(i)(v) within 15 days after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration of the disaster-related forbearance plan if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is not able to establish QRPC during the disaster related forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was current or less than 60 days delinquent (i.e., less than two
elements.label: text

elements.text: months delinquent) as of the date the Eligible Disaster occurred, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage meets all requirements described in Section 9203.4(i)(vii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9203.4(i) for complete requirements for the Disaster Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Solicitation for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is ineligible for a solicitation for a Disaster Payment Deferral as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described above, then the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a streamlined offer for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Freddie Mac Flex Modification in accordance with the special Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification requirements for Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster, as described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 9206.1(c)(v).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Otherwise, the Servicer must evaluate in accordance with regular Guide requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a streamlined offer for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification, as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206.1(c)(iii). The Servicer must send a streamlined offer for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification to an eligible Borrower within 15 days of the expiration of the forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was eligible for a solicitation for a Disaster Payment Deferral but did not
elements.label: text

elements.text: accept the offer, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a streamlined offer for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification following the same requirements as described above
elements.label: text

elements.text: except that the Servicer must send the streamlined offer to an eligible Borrower within 15
elements.label: text

elements.text: days of the expiration of the Disaster Payment Deferral offer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Complete requirements for Freddie Mac Flex Modifications for Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: impacted by an Eligible Disaster can be found in Sections 9203.4(i) and 9206.1(c)(v).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Transition requirements for Borrowers who were on a Trial Period Plan at the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower who was performing in accordance with the terms of a Trial Period Plan is
elements.label: text

elements.text: placed on forbearance as a result of an Eligible Disaster, then within 30 days prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: end of the forbearance period, the Servicer must determine whether the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial circumstances continue to be adversely impacted by the disaster based on verbal
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirmation with the Borrower about his or her current financial condition and an
elements.label: text

elements.text: updated assessment of the extent of the property damage resulting from most recent
elements.label: text

elements.text: property inspection or the current conditions of the property based on discussions with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was performing on a Freddie Mac Flex Modification at the time of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Disaster, the Servicer must make a new streamlined offer to the Borrower for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial Period Plan meeting the requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the Borrower’s financial circumstances, the Borrower must complete a new
elements.label: text

elements.text: three-month Trial Period Plan that begins immediately following the forbearance plan in
elements.label: text

elements.text: order to be eligible for a permanent modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8404.1: Servicing Mortgages impacted by a disaster
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: (Future effective date 10/01/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Overview of Servicer responsibilities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Property protection activities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Insurance loss settlements after an Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Delinquency management activities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disaster reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transition following disaster-related forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Overview of Servicer responsibilities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter provides Servicers with our requirements for assisting Borrowers whose
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises or places of employment are located in an Eligible Disaster Area.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A disaster may be a natural disaster (e.g., an earthquake, flood or hurricane) or a man-made
elements.label: text

elements.text: disaster (e.g., a civil disturbance) that adversely affects a geographical area encompassing
elements.label: text

elements.text: properties that secure Freddie Mac Mortgages or Borrowers’ places of employment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Property protection activities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: In conjunction with the requirements outlined in Section 8404.1(d), the Servicer is required
elements.label: text

elements.text: to protect the Mortgaged Premises by following the requirements outlined in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event of a disaster impacting Mortgages serviced for Freddie Mac, a Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ascertain the number of such Mortgages and the extent of the damage that may have
elements.label: text

elements.text: been caused by the disaster for each Mortgaged Premises, which may be completed by:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Determining the status of the property through discussions with the Borrower; and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Completing a property inspection per requirements outlined in Section 9202.3(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are reminded that property inspections will be reimbursed based on the
elements.label: text

elements.text: limits set forth in Section 9701.2(d). Property inspections that are completed outside
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the requirements set forth in Section 9701.2(d) may not receive reimbursement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Secure an Abandoned Property (as defined in Section 8403.1) if the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: has not sustained significant or total damage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide assistance to the Borrower regarding options for local, State or federal disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Monitor and coordinate the insurance claim process and the progress of repairs in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 8202.5
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In addition to the requirements of Section 8202.5, comply with the insurance claim
elements.label: text

elements.text: process requirements in accordance with Section 8404.1(c) for Eligible Disasters
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicer determines that the disaster has affected a Mortgaged Premises and it may pose
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Risk of Property Ownership (see Section 9202.2(b)), the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: within five Business Days of learning of the situation. In such instances, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: attach copies of the most recent six consecutive months (or less, depending on the level of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delinquency) of Form 1013, 1-4 Unit Property Inspection Report, and any other relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: information when reporting the Risk of Property Ownership to Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 8403.1(a) and 8403.1(b) for additional requirements on properties that
elements.label: text

elements.text: become distressed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Insurance loss settlements after an Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgaged Premises has been damaged and is located in an Eligible Disaster Area,
elements.label: text

elements.text: property insurance proceeds for structural losses must be disbursed in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance loss settlements requirements in Section 8202.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Delinquency management activities following a disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event a disaster strikes, it is imperative that Servicers be considerate of the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances and work to obtain quality right party contact (QRPC) with the Borrower as
elements.label: text

elements.text: soon as possible. This section will outline specific activities that a Servicer must take to
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriately manage the Borrower’s Delinquency if the Servicer believes the disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: resulted in a hardship for the Borrower and the disaster is an Eligible Disaster.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Circumstances where QRPC has not been achieved
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer has not yet achieved QRPC, then the Servicer, at its discretion, may place
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Borrower who becomes 31 or more days delinquent due to an Eligible Disaster in a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan for up to 90 days, in accordance with the requirements in this section,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the transition following disaster-related forbearance requirements in Section 8404.1(f)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the forbearance-related requirements in Sections 9203.3(a) through 9203.3(f).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower is 31 days or more delinquent at the time of impact due to an Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster, the Servicer must achieve QRPC before approving forbearance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The forbearance plan must not exceed 90 days without either achieving QRPC or
elements.label: text

elements.text: receiving approval from Freddie Mac (see Directory 5). If the Servicer has relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: information regarding the Borrower’s current financial circumstances and/or the extent of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the property damage caused by the Eligible Disaster, the Servicer should consider these
elements.label: text

elements.text: factors in its evaluation decision whether to provide forbearance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If QRPC has not been achieved by the end of the forbearance period, then the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must comply with the requirements of Section 8404.1(f)(ii), which provide the transition
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements when QRPC has not been achieved.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Circumstances where QRPC has been achieved
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower does not require any form of relief and there is no Risk of Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership (see Section 9202.2(b)) to Freddie Mac as a result of damage caused by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: disaster, Servicers should continue to service the Mortgage in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of the Guide.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: would pose a Risk of Property Ownership to Freddie Mac, Servicers have discretion to
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspend collection and foreclosure proceedings by placing the Borrower into a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan, based on the circumstances of each case. The Servicer is not required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: obtain a complete Borrower Response Package from the Borrower and may offer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Forbearance for a period of one to three months, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If necessary, one or more successive forbearance plan periods of one to three months
elements.label: text

elements.text: and which collectively may not exceed 12 months
elements.label: text

elements.text: Without prior approval from Freddie Mac, the forbearance plan may not be extended
elements.label: text

elements.text: beyond a date that would cause the Delinquency to exceed a cumulative total of 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months of the Borrower’s contractual monthly Mortgage payment, including taxes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance if the Servicer is collecting Escrow for those expenses.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When applicable, such a suspension of Servicing action must be pre-approved by the MI,
elements.label: text

elements.text: FHA, RHS or VA to avoid jeopardizing benefits of any applicable insurance or guaranty.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance period to determine if forbearance should be extended up to a total of 12
elements.label: text

elements.text: months or the hardship has been resolved.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer believes forbearance beyond a total of 12 months or beyond a total that
elements.label: text

elements.text: would cause the Borrower to exceed 12 months of delinquency is warranted, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must make that recommendation to Freddie Mac for consideration by submitting a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Forbearance Extension Request in Resolve®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event Freddie Mac participated in evaluating a Borrower for forbearance beyond a
elements.label: text

elements.text: total of 12 months and Freddie Mac denied the request, the Servicer must refer to Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1301.2(i) for more information on adverse action notices that must be provided to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower on behalf of Freddie Mac under certain limited circumstances.
elements.label: text

elements.text: At the end of the forbearance period, the Servicer must reassess the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances to determine whether the Borrower’s hardship resulting from the Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Disaster has been resolved. The determination may be based on information from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower as a result of QRPC and/or:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An updated property inspection assessment of the extent of property damage
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Discussions with the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the hardship has been resolved and the Borrower is ready to transition to a permanent
elements.label: text

elements.text: solution, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for the most appropriate workout
elements.label: text

elements.text: option to cure the Delinquency in accordance with the requirements in Section 8404.1(f).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the hardship has not been resolved, then the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: eligibility for extended forbearance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Mortgage eligibility and forbearance plan requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except as otherwise provided in this section, when offering forbearance relief to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster, Servicers must use the requirements set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Chapter 9203.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage may be secured by the following property types:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Primary Residence
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Second home, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Investment Property
elements.label: text

elements.text: The property may be vacant or condemned but not abandoned.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: may waive the requirement that the forbearance plan be in writing. In these
elements.label: text

elements.text: circumstances, the Servicer may enter the Borrower into a forbearance plan through a
elements.label: text

elements.text: verbal agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not assess late charges as long as the Borrower is on a forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan or paying as agreed on a repayment plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) Disaster reporting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Credit reporting for Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text
