elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reporting loans affected by a disaster to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must report all Mortgages that are affected by a disaster and are 31 or more
elements.label: text

elements.text: days delinquent to Freddie Mac via an EDR transmission within the first three Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Days of the month following the month the Servicer learned of the disaster using default
elements.label: text

elements.text: reason code 034 (Eligible Disaster Area).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Reporting forbearance or repayment plans to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Servicer must report all Mortgages that are subject to a repayment plan or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: forbearance plan resulting from disaster-caused hardship via Resolve as specified in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 9203.2(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 9203 for more details.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) Transition following disaster-related forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was placed on forbearance as a result of an Eligible Disaster, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must contact the Borrower on a periodic basis and prior to the end of the forbearance period
elements.label: text

elements.text: to determine whether the hardship has been resolved and the most appropriate relief or
elements.label: text

elements.text: workout option to cure the Delinquency. The Servicer must consider a number of factors,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer’s ability to achieve QRPC
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Borrower’s current financial circumstances and ability to resume making monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The status of the Mortgage at the time of the disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, at the end of the disaster-related forbearance period, the Servicer is evaluating the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower for a foreclosure prevention alternative based on a Borrower Response Package,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the documentation cannot be more than 180 days old as of the date of the evaluation for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure prevention alternative.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Evaluation hierarchy
elements.label: text

elements.text: If QRPC is established with a Borrower who was current or less than 60 days delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i.e., less than two months delinquent) as of the date the Eligible Disaster occurred, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower is unable to resolve the Delinquency through a reinstatement or repayment
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for the loss mitigation options in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following Disaster evaluation hierarchy:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Freddie Mac Flex Modification®
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Standard Short Sale
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Standard Deed-in-Lieu of Foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was not current or less than 60 days delinquent (i.e., less than two months
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent) as of the date of the Eligible Disaster, the Servicer must conduct all loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigation evaluations in accordance with the standard loss mitigation evaluation
elements.label: text

elements.text: hierarchy, as described in Section 9201.2, or must submit an exception request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In most cases, Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster who qualify to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluated for a Disaster Payment Deferral will be transitioning from a forbearance plan,
elements.label: text

elements.text: but forbearance is not a prerequisite.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Solicitation for a Disaster Payment Deferral
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must proactively solicit the Borrower for a Disaster Payment Deferral in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Section 9203.4(i)(v) within 15 days after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: expiration of the disaster-related forbearance plan if:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer is not able to establish QRPC during the disaster related forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage was current or less than 60 days delinquent (i.e., less than two
elements.label: text

elements.text: months delinquent) as of the date the Eligible Disaster occurred, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage meets all requirements described in Section 9203.4(i)(vii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 9203.4(i) for complete requirements for the Disaster Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Deferral.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Solicitation for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is ineligible for a solicitation for a Disaster Payment Deferral as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described above, then the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a streamlined offer for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Freddie Mac Flex Modification in accordance with the special Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification requirements for Borrowers impacted by an Eligible Disaster, as described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 9206.1(c)(v).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Otherwise, the Servicer must evaluate in accordance with regular Guide requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: a streamlined offer for a Freddie Mac Flex Modification, as described in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 9206.1(c)(iii). The Servicer must send a streamlined offer for a Freddie Mac Flex
elements.label: text

elements.text: Modification to an eligible Borrower within 15 days of the expiration of the forbearance
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was eligible for a solicitation for a Disaster Payment Deferral but did not
elements.label: text

elements.text: accept the offer, the Servicer must evaluate the Borrower for a streamlined offer for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification following the same requirements as described above
elements.label: text

elements.text: except that the Servicer must send the streamlined offer to an eligible Borrower within 15
elements.label: text

elements.text: days of the expiration of the Disaster Payment Deferral offer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Complete requirements for Freddie Mac Flex Modifications for Borrowers
elements.label: text

elements.text: impacted by an Eligible Disaster can be found in Sections 9203.4(i) and 9206.1(c)(v).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Transition requirements for Borrowers who were on a Trial Period Plan at the time
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Eligible Disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Borrower who was performing in accordance with the terms of a Trial Period Plan is
elements.label: text

elements.text: placed on forbearance as a result of an Eligible Disaster, then within 30 days prior to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: end of the forbearance period, the Servicer must determine whether the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial circumstances continue to be adversely impacted by the disaster based on verbal
elements.label: text

elements.text: confirmation with the Borrower about his or her current financial condition and an
elements.label: text

elements.text: updated assessment of the extent of the property damage resulting from most recent
elements.label: text

elements.text: property inspection or the current conditions of the property based on discussions with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Borrower.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower was performing on a Freddie Mac Flex Modification at the time of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Disaster, the Servicer must make a new streamlined offer to the Borrower for a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac Flex Modification Trial Period Plan meeting the requirements of Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the Borrower’s financial circumstances, the Borrower must complete a new
elements.label: text

elements.text: three-month Trial Period Plan that begins immediately following the forbearance plan in
elements.label: text

elements.text: order to be eligible for a permanent modification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8405: Occupancy Waivers
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 8405.1: 12-month occupancy waiver request (07/09/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: For Mortgages secured by a Borrower’s Primary Residence or a second home, the Fannie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mae/Freddie Mac Single-Family Uniform Security Instrument and the Second Home Rider
elements.label: text

elements.text: provides that a Borrower, except as otherwise permitted by the instrument, must occupy the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises within 60 days of executing the Security Instrument, or as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupy and use the property as the Borrower’s second home, and continue to occupy the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises, or as applicable, keep the Property available primarily as a residence for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s personal use and enjoyment, for at least one year after the date of occupancy or as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable the date of the Second Home Rider.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When Freddie Mac approval is required, the Servicer must document the Borrower’s request and
elements.label: text

elements.text: forward a recommendation to Freddie Mac via e-mail to ShortSales@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will review the request, supporting documentation and the Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: recommendation and notify the Servicer of its approval or denial of the request. The Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must maintain the Borrower’s request, supporting documentation, if applicable, and Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac’s decision in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8406: Transfers of Ownership and
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Assumptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8406.1: General policy and federal regulation on
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Transfers of Ownership and assumptions (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ General policy on Transfers of Ownership and assumptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of Ownership and assumptions of Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by
elements.label: text

elements.text: the VA or RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Federal restrictions on the exercise of the due-on-transfer clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional permitted Transfers of Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) General policy on Transfers of Ownership and assumptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) General policy on Transfers of Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership of the Mortgaged Premises securing a Mortgage that does not contain a due￾on-transfer clause can be transferred without restriction. However, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: accelerate the maturity of a Mortgage that contains a due-on-sale or due-on-transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: clause when a Transfer of Ownership occurs, unless acceleration is prohibited by
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions of Section 8406.1(c) or 8406.1(d) or applicable law. References in the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: to “due-on-transfer clause” includes “due-on-sale clauses” and similar provisions in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage documents that require acceleration upon a Transfer of Ownership.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should review the definition of Transfer of Ownership in the Glossary for a list
elements.label: text

elements.text: of impacted transactions. Servicers are reminded that when a Transfer of Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurs that involves a modified Mortgage, they must review the modification documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: in addition to the Note and Security Instrument to determine if a due-on-transfer clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been triggered. In accordance with Section 9206.2(c), such clauses must be included
elements.label: text

elements.text: in a modified Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon learning of a Transfer of Ownership that is subject to acceleration under the terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: of this chapter, the Servicer must accelerate the debt and initiate appropriate foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: action in accordance with applicable law, the terms of the Security Instrument and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 9301. No penalty may be charged for a prepayment resulting from such
elements.label: text

elements.text: acceleration.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) General policy on assumptions of delinquent Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: It may be in the best interest of Freddie Mac to permit an assumption of a delinquent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage by a creditworthy applicant, even if the Mortgage contains a due-on-transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: clause. Freddie Mac approval is necessary to approve or decline any request to assume a
elements.label: text

elements.text: delinquent Mortgage. In these instances, refer to Section 9207.1 for workout Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption requirements and contact Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) to determine
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether a workout Mortgage assumption is an appropriate alternative to foreclosure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Change in Note Rate requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not permit a change in the Note Rate upon a Transfer of Ownership of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises, except in connection with a simultaneous assumption and
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification that meets the requirements set forth in Section 9207.1(b). A Servicer that
elements.label: text

elements.text: intends to modify the Note Rate without meeting the requirements of Section 9207.1(b)
elements.label: text

elements.text: must first repurchase Freddie Mac’s interest in the Mortgage in accordance with Chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Transfers of Ownership and assumptions of Mortgages insured by the FHA or
elements.label: text

elements.text: guaranteed by the VA or RHS
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgaged Premises securing Mortgages insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: or RHS may be transferred in accordance with applicable FHA, VA or RHS regulations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must comply with all applicable FHA, VA or RHS requirements to ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the FHA insurance, or VA or RHS guaranty is maintained and must provide all notices and
elements.label: text

elements.text: disclosures required under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Truth in Lending Act and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other applicable law or regulation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac need not be notified of any change of ownership allowed by the FHA, VA or
elements.label: text

elements.text: RHS, but the Servicer must ensure that all insurance and guaranty documents reflect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: change.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When prior FHA, VA or RHS approval is required and such approval is not granted, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must provide an adverse action notice to all applicable parties, in addition to any
elements.label: text

elements.text: other notice or disclosure required under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair Credit
elements.label: text

elements.text: Reporting Act, Truth in Lending Act and any other applicable law or regulation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The application for assumption of a Mortgage insured by the FHA or guaranteed by the VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: or RHS is not complete until the Servicer receives the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The completed application for approval of a loan assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. An executed copy of the contract of sale or other document reflecting the Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership (e.g., quitclaim deed)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. With regard to (a) the continuation of the mortgage insurance provided by the FHA, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage guaranty provided by the VA or the Mortgage guaranty provided by the RHS,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as applicable, and (b) the release of the prior Borrower of liability, when applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: written approval from:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The FHA for Mortgages insured by the FHA
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The VA for Mortgages guaranteed by the VA
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The RHS for Mortgages insured by the RHS; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. All other information the FHA, VA or RHS, as applicable, may require
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Federal restrictions on the exercise of the due-on-transfer clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: For the following Transfers of Ownership, when the Mortgaged Premises is either occupied
elements.label: text

elements.text: or to be occupied by the Borrower, the Servicer may not accelerate the maturity of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: indebtedness:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The creation of a lien or other encumbrance subordinate to the lender’s Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument, which does not relate to a transfer of rights of occupancy in the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises, provided that the lien or encumbrance is not created pursuant to a contract for
elements.label: text

elements.text: deed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The creation of a purchase-money security interest for household appliances
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A transfer by devise, descent or operation of law on the death of a joint tenant or tenant
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the entirety
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The granting of a leasehold interest with a term of three years or less and without an
elements.label: text

elements.text: option to purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A transfer in which the transferee occupies or will occupy the Mortgaged Premises and
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is one of the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A transfer to a relative, resulting from the Borrower’s death
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: In the case of a Borrower’s death, the occupancy requirement may be waived.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer should submit the waiver recommendation to Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Directory 5) for approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A transfer wherein the spouse, domestic partner or a child of the transferor becomes
elements.label: text

elements.text: an owner of the Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A transfer resulting from a decree of dissolution of a marriage or domestic
elements.label: text

elements.text: partnership, a legal separation agreement or from an incidental property settlement
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement by which the spouse or domestic partner becomes an owner of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A transfer into an inter vivo trust in which the Borrower is and remains the beneficiary
elements.label: text

elements.text: and occupant of the Mortgaged Premises unless, as a condition precedent to such a
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer, the Borrower refuses to provide the Servicer with reasonable means acceptable
elements.label: text

elements.text: to the Servicer by which the Servicer will be assured of timely notice of any subsequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer of the beneficial interest or change in occupancy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any other transfer or disposition described in regulations of the Federal Home Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Bank Board as a basis on which due-on-transfer clauses may not be exercised
elements.label: text

elements.text: Except for the waiver of occupancy requirements for transferees as described above,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may not evaluate the creditworthiness of a transferee, require a transferee to
elements.label: text

elements.text: assume the Mortgage or otherwise require the Servicer’s or Freddie Mac’s approval for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Transfers of Ownership types listed in this section.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the transferee requests to assume the Mortgage and/or the transferor requests to be released
elements.label: text

elements.text: from liability, then the Servicer must determine the creditworthiness of the transferee in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Sections 8406.2(a) and 8406.2(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Additional permitted Transfers of Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Unrestricted Transfers of Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the federal restrictions on the exercise of the due-on-transfer clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in Section 8406.1(c), Freddie Mac will not, and the Servicer may not, restrict a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Ownership of the Mortgaged Premises in the following situations:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Security Instrument does not contain a due-on-transfer clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Security Instrument contains an unenforceable due-on-transfer clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The title is acquired by the junior lienholder (that is an institutional counterparty) as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the result of a foreclosure or acceptance of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure of the junior
elements.label: text

elements.text: lien. However, Freddie Mac will require acceleration of the indebtedness if the title is
elements.label: text

elements.text: further transferred after acquisition by the junior lienholder; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The title to the leasehold estate and the improvements are acquired by the fee simple
elements.label: text

elements.text: landowner/lessor as the result of the Borrower being evicted from the leasehold estate
elements.label: text

elements.text: by court order. However, Freddie Mac will require acceleration of the indebtedness if
elements.label: text

elements.text: the title is further transferred to a new lessee after acquisition by the lessor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with any Transfer of Ownership listed in this Section 8406.1(d)(i), a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may not evaluate the creditworthiness of a transferee, require a transferee to
elements.label: text

elements.text: assume the Mortgage, or otherwise require the Servicer’s or Freddie Mac’s approval of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the transfer. However, if the transferee requests to assume the Mortgage and/or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferor requests to be released of liability, the Servicer must determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: creditworthiness of the transferee, either manually or by using Loan Product Advisor®.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 8406.2(a) and 8406.2(b) for additional information on Transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Ownership.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Permitted Transfers of Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: In situations where all of the following conditions are met, Freddie Mac will permit a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transfer of Ownership of the Mortgaged Premises:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least 12 months have passed since the Origination Date
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer has complied with all mortgage insurance requirements applicable to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The transfer is to a transferee who occupies or will occupy the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises as a Primary Residence and is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A parent or child of the transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A grandparent or grandchild of the transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A brother or sister of the transferor, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An original co-Borrower of the transferor under the Note, whether or not
elements.label: text

elements.text: related to the transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The transfer is to a limited liability company (LLC) or limited partnership (LP),
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The managing member/general partner of the LLC/LP is the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower. If there are multiple Borrowers, all of them must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: members/partners of the LLC/LP, and at least one of them must be a
elements.label: text

elements.text: managing member/general partner. If the transfer results in a permitted change
elements.label: text

elements.text: of occupancy type to an investment property, such change must not violate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument (e.g., the 12-month occupancy requirement for a Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicer notifies the original owner or natural person that the Mortgaged
elements.label: text

elements.text: Premises transferred to an LLC/LP must be transferred back to the original
elements.label: text

elements.text: owner or natural person prior to any subsequent refinance or modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: application to meet Freddie Mac’s underwriting requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The Mortgage is a HeritageOne® Mortgage, and the transfer is to the Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Native American Tribe (as defined in Section 4504.2(c)) within whose Tribal
elements.label: text

elements.text: Area the Mortgaged Premises are located if, after acquisition, the Eligible Native
elements.label: text

elements.text: American Tribe further transfers or will transfer the title to a transferee who
elements.label: text

elements.text: occupies or will occupy the Mortgaged Premises as a Primary Residence, and at
elements.label: text

elements.text: least one transferee is also an enrolled member of a Native American Tribe (as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 8901.1(a))
elements.label: text

elements.text: In connection with any Transfer of Ownership that meets the conditions of this Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8406.1(d)(ii), a Servicer may not evaluate the creditworthiness of a transferee, require a
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferee to assume the Mortgage or otherwise require the Servicer’s or Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval of the transfer. However, if the transferee requests to assume the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or the transferor requests to be released of liability, the Servicer must determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: creditworthiness of the transferee, either manually or by using Loan Product Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Sections 8406.2(a) and 8406.2(b) for additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Transfers of Ownership that require a determination of creditworthiness
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the following situations, the Servicer must determine the creditworthiness of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferee, even if the transferee is not assuming the Mortgage, and process the Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Ownership in accordance with Sections 8406.2(a) through 8406.3(b):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Security Instrument contains a due-on-transfer clause that does not allow
elements.label: text

elements.text: unrestricted or automatic acceleration of the indebtedness upon transfer of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises to a creditworthy transferee, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The title is acquired by the junior lienholder (that is not an institutional counterparty)
elements.label: text

elements.text: as the result of a foreclosure or acceptance of a deed-in-lieu of foreclosure of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: junior lien. However, Freddie Mac will require acceleration of the indebtedness if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: title is further transferred after acquisition by the junior lienholder.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv) Assumptions, releases of liability and determination of creditworthiness
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the transferee requests an assumption of the Mortgage obligation as part of a Transfer
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Ownership, the Servicer must determine the creditworthiness of the transferee in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the manual underwriting requirements in Topics 5100 through 5500. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must prepare a written assumption agreement that must be executed by all
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties concerned as required in Section 8406.2(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition, if the transferor requests to be released of liability, once the Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: determined the creditworthiness of the transferee, the Servicer must require a written
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption and release of liability agreement, which then must be executed by all parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: concerned in accordance with Section 8406.2(c).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must accelerate the Note if title is further transferred by the transferee to
elements.label: text

elements.text: someone who is not an eligible transferee under Sections 8406.1(c) or 8406.1(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8406.2: Eligibility and application for approval of
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Transfers of Ownership, assumptions and releases of
elements.label: text

elements.text: liability (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Application for approval of Transfers of Ownership, assumptions and releases of liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining eligibility for Transfers of Ownership, assumptions and releases of liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approval of the application for Transfers of Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adverse action notice requirements for declined Mortgage assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Application for approval of Transfers of Ownership, assumptions and releases of
elements.label: text

elements.text: liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Transfers of Ownership that require the Servicer to first determine the creditworthiness
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the transferee, and for all assumptions and releases of liability, the Servicer must obtain
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Form 65, Uniform Residential Loan Application, completed and executed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A copy of the executed contract of sale or other document reflecting the Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership (e.g., quitclaim deed)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A current credit report for the transferee, as described in Section 5203.1
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Income documentation and asset documentation for the transferee as required by Topics
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5100 through 5500
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ In accordance with requirements in Chapters 5302 through 5307 and Chapter 5501 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: standard documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Written verification of the transferee’s current employment and income from a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reliable source and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Verification or other acceptable evidence of the source and amount of funds for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Down Payment and payment of prepaid items, as reported in the application
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Section 8406.2(b) for underwriting requirements to determine whether a
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferee is creditworthy.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must comply with the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair Credit Reporting Act,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Truth in Lending Act and any other applicable law or regulation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See also Section 1301.2 for additional compliance requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must also notify the transferee of the decision on the application within 25 days
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Servicer receives all information that constitutes a completed application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Determining eligibility for Transfers of Ownership, assumptions and releases of liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Manually reviewing transferee eligibility
elements.label: text

elements.text: When required to determine the creditworthiness of a transferee for assumptions and
elements.label: text

elements.text: releases of liability, and when manually underwriting a Transfer of Ownership following
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements of Section 8406.1(d), the Servicer must review the application to ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with Freddie Mac’s credit underwriting guidelines in Topics 5100 through
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5500 including, but not limited to, income and employment, source of funds for Down
elements.label: text

elements.text: Payment and Closing Costs and credit history. The Servicer’s analysis must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented on Form 1077, Uniform Underwriting and Transmittal Summary, or on
elements.label: text

elements.text: another document in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Use of Loan Product Advisor® to assess creditworthiness for Transfers of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership and Mortgage assumptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgage assumptions that require the Servicer to first determine the
elements.label: text

elements.text: creditworthiness of the transferee as described in Section 8406.1(d)(iv), Servicers may
elements.label: text

elements.text: use Loan Product Advisor to assess the creditworthiness of the transferee(s) as described
elements.label: text

elements.text: in this section. This functionality is permitted only for transferees who request an
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption of the Mortgage obligation as part of a Transfer of Ownership and must only
elements.label: text

elements.text: be used to assess creditworthiness of the transferee. All other Guide requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: regarding Transfers of Ownership and Mortgage assumptions remain in effect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers that opt to use Loan Product Advisor to assess a transferee’s creditworthiness to
elements.label: text

elements.text: process assumptions in Loan Product Advisor must use the “no cash-out” refinance
elements.label: text

elements.text: option and the “Assumption FRE Owned Loan” Loan Program Identifier.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor uses the Mortgage’s original value to assess the transferee’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: creditworthiness; therefore, Servicers have the flexibility to manually underwrite an
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption for any reason, including if the transferee requests use of the Mortgage’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: current value with a BPO obtained via BPODirect.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Feedback Certificate returned from the Servicer’s last submission to Loan Product
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor prior to the Servicer’s assumption determination (the Last Assumption Feedback
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate) returns a Feedback Certificate with a Risk Class of “Accept”, the Servicer is
elements.label: text

elements.text: not required to manually underwrite the assumption of the Mortgage in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: the requirements in Topics 5100 through 5500. To move forward with the assumption,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must follow the steps in Sections 8406.2(c) through 8406.3(b), as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: to complete the assumption and must maintain the Last Assumption Feedback Certificate
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If Loan Product Advisor returns a Feedback Certificate with a Risk Class of “Caution”,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer may manually underwrite the assumption of the Mortgage in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements in Topics 5100 through 5500. If the Servicer ultimately declines
elements.label: text

elements.text: the assumption application, the Servicer must follow the requirements in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8406.2(d) to notify all affected parties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Loan Product Advisor may be used to assess the creditworthiness of transferees who
elements.label: text

elements.text: want to assume a Mortgage with a previous Payment Deferral; however, it may not be
elements.label: text

elements.text: used to assess the creditworthiness of transferees who want to assume a Mortgage that
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been previously modified. Modified Mortgages are eligible to be assumed, but they
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be manually underwritten using the manual underwriting requirements in Topics
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5100 through 5500.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Optional use of Loan Product Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers may continue to process Mortgage assumptions manually if they choose to do
elements.label: text

elements.text: so or at the request of the transferee.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements must be met for all assumptions, when applicable:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The transferee must be determined to be creditworthy in accordance with Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The assumption is manually underwritten, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If Loan Product Advisor was used to assess Transferee creditworthiness, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption is one for which Borrower creditworthiness is permitted to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessed through Loan Product Advisor and the Last Assumption Feedback
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certificate includes a Risk Class of Accept
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A written assumption and release of liability agreement must be executed by all
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties concerned in accordance with Section 8406.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Freddie Mac will require acceleration of the indebtedness if title is further
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferred by the transferee to someone who is not an eligible transferee under
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. If the Mortgage is being assumed, the transferee must pay at least 5% of the sale price
elements.label: text

elements.text: in addition to all adjustments (e.g., for property taxes, and property and mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance premiums) from cash or other equity as defined in Chapter 5501. (Note:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The transferee is not required to pay 5% for those Transfers of Ownership protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: by federal restrictions on exercise of the due-on-transfer clause as set forth in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8406.1(c) or for those Transfers of Ownership permitted under Sections 8406.1(d)(i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: and 8406.1(d)(ii), even if, in those cases, the transferee is also assuming the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. There must be no changes in the terms of the Security Instrument and the Note other
elements.label: text

elements.text: than a change to the name of the transferee, any release and subsequent assumption of
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal liability, where applicable, and any change allowed in Section 8103.3(d)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Mortgage insurance coverage must be maintained, if applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Mortgaged Premises to be transferred secures a Mortgage that is covered by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown agreement, the Servicer must underwrite the transferee according to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: qualification requirements provided in Section 4204.3. See Section 8406.3(d) for
elements.label: text

elements.text: additional information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Approval of the application for Transfers of Ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must approve the Transfer of Ownership if, based on its eligibility review in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with Section 8406.2(b), it reasonably determines that the security interest in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises will not be impaired by this transfer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer is not required to notify Freddie Mac of the Transfer of Ownership permitted
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Sections 8406.1(c) and 8406.1(d), provided the Mortgage is not assumed by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferee or the transferor is not being released of liability, unless it becomes necessary later
elements.label: text

elements.text: to communicate with Freddie Mac concerning this Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must prepare the necessary documents and have them executed by all parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: concerned. In addition, if the transferor requests to be released of liability under the Note and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument, a written assumption and release of liability agreement must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: executed. If State law requires Freddie Mac to execute the documents, the Servicer warrants
elements.label: text

elements.text: that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The documents reflect terms that are consistent with the requirements of this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All statements set forth in the documents are accurate, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The documents comply with all applicable State and local requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deliver the original executed assumption agreement and, as applicable, release of liability
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement, to the Document Custodian; and copies to all other parties concerned
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Per Section 1402.5(b)(ii), the Servicer must update the MERS® eRegistry (as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined in Section 1402.1(b)) to provide notice of the assumption agreement upon an
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption (with or without a release of liability) of an eMortgage (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retain a copy of the executed assumption agreement and, as applicable, release of
elements.label: text

elements.text: liability agreement, in the Mortgage file
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Arrange for any recordation commonly required by private institutional Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: investors or required by law to ensure the priority of the existing lien and assess any
elements.label: text

elements.text: related costs to the transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide all notices and disclosures required under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair
elements.label: text

elements.text: Credit Reporting Act, Truth in Lending Act and any other applicable law or regulation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see also Section 1301.2 for additional compliance requirements)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that all insurance policies reflect the change of ownership and take any action
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary to continue any required insurance coverage including, mortgage insurance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: where applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document transferee information in the Freddie Mac Post-Fund Data Correction tool (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Exhibit 88, Servicing Tools). Servicer must submit copies of the settlement statement and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption agreement in accordance with instructions for use of the tool. If Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: needs to submit more than one request to change information, Servicer may import the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Post-Fund Data Correction Form excel spreadsheet (DCR Form) found on the Post-Fund
elements.label: text

elements.text: Data Correction tool webpage (https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/technology￾tools/our-solutions/post-fund-data-correction) into the Post-Fund Data Correction tool,
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with the spreadsheet instructions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Cooperative Share Loans, comply with the requirements in Section 8801.3(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: When applicable, the Servicer may cancel mortgage insurance following the Transfer of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Ownership of the Mortgaged Premises in accordance with Sections 8203.2 through
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer is unable to approve the Transfer of Ownership or assumption following
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide requirements and believes that approval is in the Borrower’s and Freddie Mac’s best
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest, the Servicer must send the request for approval to ShortSales@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Adverse action notice requirements for declined Mortgage assumption
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the application for a Mortgage assumption is declined, the Servicer must provide an
elements.label: text

elements.text: adverse action notice to all applicable parties, or any other notice or disclosure required
elements.label: text

elements.text: under the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair Credit Reporting Act, Truth in Lending Act and
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other applicable federal, State or local law. Freddie Mac must not be identified as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: creditor in any notice or disclosure that may be required, as Freddie Mac is not participating
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the decision on the application.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8406.3: File retention, fees and special provisions for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Transfers of Ownership and assumptions (09/10/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ File retention for Transfers of Ownership and assumptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer of Ownership fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assumability provisions and rate cap requirements for ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfers of Ownership and buydown accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) File retention for Transfers of Ownership and assumptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: All original documents relating to the application for a Transfer of Ownership must be kept
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Mortgage file, including the Last Assumption Feedback Certificate, as defined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8406.2(b), if Loan Product Advisor® was used to assess Transferee creditworthiness.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the application is approved, the documentation must be maintained in the Mortgage file for
elements.label: text

elements.text: at least seven years from the date that Freddie Mac’s interest in the Mortgage is satisfied.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the application is declined, the documentation must be kept for the duration required under
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Equal Credit Opportunity Act, Fair Credit Reporting Act or any other applicable law or
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Copies of documents may be scanned and stored as:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Portable Document Format (PDF)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Tagged Image File (TIF) format
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Joint Photograph Experts Group (JPEG) format; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other electronic document formats commonly used by the Mortgage industry in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: regular course of business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Chapter 3302 for Mortgage file retention requirements including those
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements regarding documents for which the paper originals must be maintained.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Transfer of Ownership fees
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may charge and retain a fee for processing a Transfer of Ownership of, or an
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in, the Mortgaged Premises securing a Mortgage purchased by Freddie Mac as
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted by applicable law. Where applicable law or regulation limits the amount of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: allowable fee, the Servicer may not charge more than the lesser of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The maximum fee allowed under applicable law or regulation, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The maximum fee allowed by Freddie Mac as stated below
elements.label: text

elements.text: This fee must be set in accordance with the following provisions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Security Instruments without a due-on-transfer clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: The fee charged by the Servicer for changing its records for a Transfer of Ownership for
elements.label: text

elements.text: which its consent was not required must not exceed $100.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Security Instruments with a due-on-transfer clause
elements.label: text

elements.text: When determining the creditworthiness of a transferee for a Transfer of Ownership, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may charge and retain a fee based on the following guidelines:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The fee may not exceed the greater of $400 or 1% of the UPB, capped at a maximum
elements.label: text

elements.text: fee of $900
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Transfer fee excludes out-of-pocket expenses paid to independent third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: for services required to comply with the application and approval process (per
elements.label: text

elements.text: sections 8406.2(a) through 8406.2(d)) and to record the change of ownership
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any additional services requested by the transferee, such as surveys, owner’s title
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies, etc., are the sole responsibility of the transferee
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan discount fees, yield enhancement fees, lost opportunity fees and similar devices
elements.label: text

elements.text: designed to circumvent these limitations or increase the effective interest rate or yield
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Mortgage previously sold to Freddie Mac, regardless of the Servicer’s retained
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest in the Mortgage, are expressly prohibited
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer may implement a transfer fee schedule that results in charges less than those
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted by Freddie Mac. However, if any fee charged by the Servicer on Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: sold to Freddie Mac exceeds the amounts stated above, it will be considered a breach of
elements.label: text

elements.text: this Guide and, unless refunded to the Borrower, Freddie Mac may exercise any remedy
elements.label: text

elements.text: available under this Guide or applicable law to remediate the violation.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Assumability provisions and rate cap requirements for ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Assumability provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The loan instruments used to originate an ARM contain the provisions governing the
elements.label: text

elements.text: right of the lender to accelerate the loan, in the event of a Transfer of Ownership. These
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions determine whether or not the ARM is assumable by the party to whom the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is transferred, and, if it is assumable, the transferee must agree to
elements.label: text

elements.text: assume the Mortgage loan obligation. Some loan instruments provide that the ARM is
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumable for the life of the loan. Other loan instruments provide that the ARM is
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumable only after the initial fixed-rate period has expired or until a specified event has
elements.label: text

elements.text: occurred and is thereafter not assumable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must review the loan instruments to determine the assumption provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: for an ARM. Regardless of whether a loan instrument indicates that an ARM is not
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumable, the ARM may be assumed as a workout Mortgage assumption or a
elements.label: text

elements.text: simultaneous modification and assumption. Servicers must refer to and follow the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in Section 9207.1 for these assumptions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Rate cap requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Note for an ARM sold to Freddie Mac does not provide for a Lifetime Ceiling, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following provision or substantially similar provision must be included in the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumption and release of liability agreement:
elements.label: text

elements.text: The interest rate I am required to pay after I assume this Mortgage obligation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the entire term of this Mortgage will never be greater than (see note below)
elements.label: text

elements.text: percent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: To determine the appropriate interest rate to insert in the preceding provision, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must add six percentage points to the sum of the Margin and the Index value in
elements.label: text

elements.text: effect on the date of the Mortgage assumption and release of liability agreement, subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable law, such as the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA). Refer to Chapter 8503.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Transfers of ownership and buydown accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: Where a Transfer of Ownership is approved, any funds remaining in a related buydown
elements.label: text

elements.text: account may continue to be used to reduce the Mortgage payments when:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgaged Premises is sold and the Mortgage is assumed by the purchaser, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The terms of the original buydown plan allow for the continued application of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: buydown funds if the Mortgage is assumed
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8501: Special Requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Servicing Step-Rate Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8501.1: Servicer staff requirements related to
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Servicing Step-Rate Mortgages (07/09/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must employ staff, including phone agents for both incoming and outgoing calls,
elements.label: text

elements.text: that is adequately trained to discuss interest rate adjustments for Step-Rate Mortgages. Phone
elements.label: text

elements.text: agents answering incoming calls must be able to identify potential default situations and
elements.label: text

elements.text: promptly refer Borrowers to a default management unit with the ability to advise on default
elements.label: text

elements.text: prevention options. Phone agents making outgoing calls must be able to discuss step-rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustments when making calls to delinquent Borrowers in accordance with Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: solicitation and communication requirements in Section 9102.5.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8501.2: Communicating upcoming interest rate
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: adjustments (07/09/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notice as early as the 150th day and no later than the 90th day prior to the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date following the initial interest rate adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notice as early as the 75th day and no later than the 60
elements.label: text

elements.text: th day prior to the first payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Due Date following the initial interest rate adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notice as early as the 120th day and no later than the 60th day prior to the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date at each subsequent adjusted rate level
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must send two separate written notifications to the Borrower prior to an initial
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest step-rate adjustment on a Step-Rate Mortgage. These notifications must be sent in order
elements.label: text

elements.text: to ensure that Borrowers are fully prepared for increases to their PITIAS Payment amounts and
elements.label: text

elements.text: have a full understanding of when and why the payment amount will increase. If after the initial
elements.label: text

elements.text: step-rate adjustment the Mortgage will be subject to subsequent interest rate adjustments, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer, in accordance with Section 8501.2(c) below, must send one notification letter prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the payment Due Date associated with each subsequent adjustment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Written notice as early as the 150th day and no later than the 90th day prior to the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date following the initial interest rate adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide the Borrower with a written notice of the initial interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustment that will occur as a result of a step-rate feature on the Borrower’s Step-Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage (i.e., at the end of a fixed-rate term).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Written notice as early as the 75th day and no later than the 60th day prior to the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date following the initial interest rate adjustment
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must provide the Borrower with a second written notice of the initial interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: rate adjustment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Each written notice of an upcoming interest rate adjustment sent to the Borrower in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the requirements in Section 8501.2(a), 8501.2(b) or 8501.2(c) must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide the amount and effective date of the interest rate increase, and the amount and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Due Date of the Borrower’s first increased monthly payment at the new adjusted level
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Explain that, pursuant to the terms of the modification agreement, at the end of the initial
elements.label: text

elements.text: fixed-rate term, the interest rate will increase according to the schedule in the agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: until it reaches a pre-determined Interest Rate Cap
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: As applicable, the Servicer must explain how the Interest Rate Cap was derived,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and that once the interest rate reaches its cap, it will remain fixed for the remaining
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage term.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Explain that the monthly payment includes an Escrow for property taxes, hazard
elements.label: text

elements.text: insurance and other escrowed expenses, which could also increase the monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: amount if those amounts are increased
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Explain how the new payment is determined
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include a payment schedule table similar to the one included in the Borrower’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: modification agreement, which outlines the future interest rates and monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: amounts (identifying principal and interest, as well as Escrows), and the effective dates
elements.label: text

elements.text: for each; alternatively, the Servicer may explain these terms, dates and amounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include the Servicer’s contact information and instructions for the Borrower to contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer if the Borrower has any questions regarding the content of the notice
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Include the Homeowners HOPE™ Hotline Number (888-995-HOPE)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Explain that free budgeting assistance from the HUD-approved housing counseling
elements.label: text

elements.text: agencies is available via HUD.gov; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide information regarding the availability of additional information on Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage Help Resource Center website (http://myhome.freddiemac.com/)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Written notice as early as the 120th day and no later than the 60th day prior to the first
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment Due Date at each subsequent adjusted rate level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must provide each Borrower with a written notice of each subsequent interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: adjustment that will occur as a result of a step-rate feature on a Step-Rate Mortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Step-Rate
