elements.text: Chapter 8502: Special Servicing and Reporting
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Requirements for ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8502.1: ARM Servicing and reporting (08/13/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: An ARM has a Note Rate that adjusts periodically based on a specified Index. The specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: features of an ARM vary depending upon the terms of the Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All ARMs must be accounted for and reported under the net yield accounting reporting method,
elements.label: text

elements.text: as described in Section 8301.2(a), and are reported via the transmission of the Loan-Level
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transaction. Under this method, Freddie Mac receives its proportionate share of principal and
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest collected each month including any Negative Amortization activity for the period. For
elements.label: text

elements.text: purposes of reporting to Freddie Mac, interest is computed by multiplying the Ending UPB of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: current Accounting Cycle of a loan by the Accounting Net Yield (ANY), using a 30-day
elements.label: text

elements.text: month/360-day year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: See Chapters 8301 through 8304 for the accounting and reporting requirements for ARMs.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Chapter 8303 for remittance requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8502.2: Rate changes on ARMs and notification
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: requirements (07/09/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Determining the new Note Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notifying the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notification of the new Accounting Net Yield (ANY)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Determining the new Note Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must adjust the Note Rate on an ARM in accordance with the provisions set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in the Note and any applicable Adjustable-Rate Riders. The Note will specify the Index
elements.label: text

elements.text: on which any adjustments must be based, as well as other factors that must be used to
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine the new Note Rate. These factors include, but are not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The dates on which the Note Rate may change (Interest Change Date)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The date on which the Index value used to calculate the new Note Rate is determined,
elements.label: text

elements.text: usually expressed as a number of days (known as the Lookback Period)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Margin, which is the number of percentage points that must be added to the current
elements.label: text

elements.text: Index value to establish the new Note Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The limitations on the amount that the Note Rate may change at the first adjustment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: subsequent adjustments and during the life of the Mortgage (Initial Cap, Periodic Cap and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Life Cap) and any associated rounding rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon each Note Rate change, the Servicer must calculate a new monthly payment amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: based on the new Note Rate that is sufficient to fully amortize the UPB of the ARM over the
elements.label: text

elements.text: remaining term. Prepayments on ARMs will only be accepted if the Index is available for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: DDLPI reported that corresponds to the prepayment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Notifying the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to each ARM serviced for Freddie Mac, the Servicer must correctly calculate
elements.label: text

elements.text: any and all adjustments to the interest rate or the monthly payment and give to the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: all notices of adjustment of interest rate and monthly payments in strict accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements of applicable law and with the terms of the Note and the Security Instrument.
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to ARMs, in the notices of adjustment in the interest rate and monthly payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided the Borrower before the effective date of the change, the Servicer must inform the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower that the Index value used to calculate the new interest rate is:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For LIBOR-indexed ARMs with interest rate adjustments based on a lookback date after
elements.label: text

elements.text: July 3, 2023, as applicable and as published on the website of Refinitiv Limited
elements.label: text

elements.text: (Refinitiv):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ FTSE USD IBOR Consumer Cash Fallback 1-Month*
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ FTSE USD IBOR Consumer Cash Fallback 6-Month* or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ FTSE USD IBOR Consumer Cash Fallback 12-Month*
elements.label: text

elements.text: *The following disclaimer has been provided by London Stock Exchange Group plc and Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: takes no responsibility for its contents: FTSE USD IBOR Cash Fallbacks are provided by London
elements.label: text

elements.text: Stock Exchange Group plc (“LSEG”) and its affiliates. LSEG and its affiliates shall not be liable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: any errors or delays in providing or making available the data contained within this service or for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: actions taken in reliance on the same. FTSE USD IBOR Cash Fallbacks cannot be used for any
elements.label: text

elements.text: commercial purpose (including redistribution) without a license. Please contact LSEG D&A
elements.label: text

elements.text: (formerly Refinitiv) if a license is required. FTSE USD IBOR Cash Fallbacks are subject to LSEG’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: terms of use and disclaimer available at https://www.lseg.com/en/ftse-russell/benchmarks/usd￾ibor-cash-fallbacks.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For SOFR-indexed ARMs, the 30-day Average SOFR as published by, or on the website
elements.label: text

elements.text: of, the Federal Reserve Bank of New York
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For Constant Maturity Treasury (CMT)-indexed ARMs, the 1-year Weekly CMT, 3-Year
elements.label: text

elements.text: Weekly CMT, and 5-Year Weekly CMT, as applicable, as published on the website of, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: otherwise made available by, the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System (i.e.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: H.15 Release - Selected Interest Rates)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Notification of the new ANY
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will notify the Servicer via the ARM notification report of the revised ANY that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer will be required to send us on an ARM in accordance with the product
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements set forth at the time of purchase. The ARM notification report will be available
elements.label: text

elements.text: through the Freddie Mac Service Loan Level Reporting tool (see Exhibit 88, Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Tools).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must verify the rate change notification information. If there are any differences
elements.label: text

elements.text: or omissions, the Servicer must notify Freddie Mac of the discrepancy via the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Data Corrections tool (see Exhibit 88) within the same Accounting Cycle the ARM
elements.label: text

elements.text: notification report is published in the Freddie Mac Loan Level Reporting tool. Do not return
elements.label: text

elements.text: the notification if the information on it is correct and complete.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The new ANY must be used for the Accounting Cycle date indicated in the notification. In
elements.label: text

elements.text: the month of the Interest Change Date, the Servicer must report the forecasted scheduled
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest for the next Accounting Cycle based on the new rate. The Servicer will be liable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: all net yield interest deficiencies that result from differences in calculations between the
elements.label: text

elements.text: ANY that Freddie Mac provides the Servicer and the Servicer’s calculations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Depending on the next Interest Change Date at the time of Mortgage delivery, the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: may not receive notification from Freddie Mac prior to the effective date of the new ANY.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8502.3: Negative Amortization (07/09/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Negative Amortization results whenever the amount of monthly interest calculated at the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: rate exceeds the Borrower’s scheduled monthly interest payment to the Servicer. Increases and
elements.label: text

elements.text: decreases in Negative Amortization are to be accounted for in the calculation of principal due
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac for each Accounting Cycle as set forth in Chapter 8301.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8503: Servicing Requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Borrowers who are Servicemembers and their
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dependents
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8503.1: Definitions related to Servicing loans, relief
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: and workout options for Servicemembers and their
elements.label: text

elements.text: Dependents (07/09/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Definitions related to Servicing Mortgages of Servicemembers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Relief options exclusive to Servicemembers and their Dependents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other relief and workout options for Servicemembers and their Dependents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Definitions related to Servicing Mortgages of Servicemembers
elements.label: text

elements.text: As used in this chapter, the following terms shall have the same definitions as those provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the Servicemembers Civil Relief Act (SCRA) and any applicable regulations as they shall
elements.label: text

elements.text: be amended from time to time:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Dependent
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Military Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Period of Military Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicemember
elements.label: text

elements.text: The term Permanent Change of Station (PCS) shall have the same meaning as that provided
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Joint Federal Travel Regulations, Volume 1, promulgated by the United States
elements.label: text

elements.text: Department of Defense, Defense Management Travel Office.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The term “State Military Relief Law” shall mean any State law that provides benefits,
elements.label: text

elements.text: protections or relief to members of the armed forces relating to a Mortgage. For example, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: not by way of limitation, the New Jersey Soldiers’ and Sailors’ Civil Relief Act (N.J.S.A. §§
elements.label: text

elements.text: 38:23C-1 et seq.), is a State Military Relief Law. State Military Relief Laws generally extend
elements.label: text

elements.text: SCRA protections to individuals not covered by the SCRA and therefore afford greater
elements.label: text

elements.text: protections than the SCRA provides.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Relief options exclusive to Servicemembers and their Dependents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Servicer responsibilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers must comply with the SCRA and all State Military Relief Laws as they apply to
elements.label: text

elements.text: any Borrower or Mortgage. The Servicer must be familiar and comply with all the
elements.label: text

elements.text: provisions of the SCRA and State Military Relief Laws and monitor changes to such laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: to ensure continuing compliance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In cases where military orders are required, Servicers may accept the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: alternative forms of documentation:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When provisions of the SCRA require a Servicemember to provide a copy of his or
elements.label: text

elements.text: her military orders*, Servicers may accept:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Official military orders that reflect the start date of the Period of Military Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A certification, including a certificate obtained from the Defense Manpower Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: Center, that delineates the start date of a Servicemember’s Period of Military
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A letter that is on official letterhead from the military unit to which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemember is assigned, is signed by the Servicemember’s commanding
elements.label: text

elements.text: officer and contains the following: A statement that the letter is intended to be
elements.label: text

elements.text: relied upon by creditors for SCRA purposes, the Servicemember’s full name and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Social Security Number, home address, start and end dates of the Period of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Military Service and commander’s contact telephone number; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Any document the Department of Defense deems a substitute for official orders
elements.label: text

elements.text: *References to “military orders” in the Guide include the alternative documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: outlined above that Servicers may accept in lieu of official military orders.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicers may accept written requests for the SCRA interest rate relief via facsimile,
elements.label: text

elements.text: U.S. mail, or electronic submission, such as e-mail or transmitting an online form
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicers may, but are not required to, accept an oral request for SCRA interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: relief if the Servicer has established processes, policies and procedures that, at a
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum, positively identify the requestor and enable the Servicer to record the
elements.label: text

elements.text: request and make the recording available to Freddie Mac upon request
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicers must submit a request for SCRA interest reimbursement, in accordance with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 8503.2(d), within 24 months of the end date of a Servicemember’s Period of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Military Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not reimburse Servicers for SCRA interest rate differential requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: when the amount requested exceeds the amount of interest represented by the Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Rate minus 6%.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Borrower eligibility for SCRA protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: The SCRA applies to any Borrower who is a Servicemember during a Period of Military
elements.label: text

elements.text: Service if the following conditions are met:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Mortgage is the contractual obligation of the Servicemember as the Borrower or
elements.label: text

elements.text: co-Borrower under the Note; or as obligee under a written agreement by which the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemember assumes the Borrower’s liability under the Note and the Borrower is
elements.label: text

elements.text: released from such liability, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Servicemember executed the Note or the assumption agreement before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting date or effective date of the Servicemember’s Period of Military Service as
elements.label: text

elements.text: shown in military orders
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: Refer to Sections 8503.2(a) through 8503.2(g) for applying SCRA provisions on
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Foreclosure relief extended to Servicemembers and their Dependents by Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac in addition to SCRA provisions and State Military Relief Laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac offers foreclosure relief in addition to that provided by the SCRA or State
elements.label: text

elements.text: Military Relief Laws. Servicers must not initiate or, if already initiated, must not
elements.label: text

elements.text: complete foreclosure of a Mortgage where a Servicemember is a Borrower, and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises is the Primary Residence of the Servicemember, regardless of when
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgage was originated, during his or her Period of Military Service or for one year
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the date Military Service ends.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Additionally, Servicers must not initiate or, if already initiated, must not complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure on a Mortgage, during a Servicemember’s Period of Military Service or for
elements.label: text

elements.text: one year after the date Military Service ends, when the Borrower:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is a Dependent of a Servicemember and the Mortgaged Premises is the Primary
elements.label: text

elements.text: Residence of the Servicemember or a Dependent of a Servicemember; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Was a Servicemember who died during the Period of Military Service and the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgaged Premises continues to serve as the Primary Residence of a Dependent of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicemember
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: If the Borrower is the spouse of a Servicemember who left Military Service on
elements.label: text

elements.text: January 1st, and the Mortgaged Premises are the Primary Residence of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemember or the spouse, then the Servicer must not initiate or complete foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: until after January 1st of the following year.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicer believes that there are unusual circumstances that warrant initiating or
elements.label: text

elements.text: completing foreclosure prior to the time allowed by this subsection 8503.1(b)(iii), such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Mortgaged Premises being vacant or abandoned, the Servicer must contact Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac (see Directory 5) to obtain prior written approval to initiate or complete foreclosure
elements.label: text

elements.text: proceedings.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report a Servicemember who is receiving relief from Freddie Mac as
elements.label: text

elements.text: outlined in this subsection 8503.1(b)(iii) as “paying as agreed” to any consumer reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: agency to which the Servicer reports.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Other relief and workout options for Servicemembers and their Dependents
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicemember or Dependent experiences an eligible hardship, such as receipt of PCS
elements.label: text

elements.text: orders, and does not qualify for protection under the provisions of the SCRA or State
elements.label: text

elements.text: Military Relief Laws, or qualifies but chooses to explore other options, the Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate the Servicemember for the most appropriate relief or workout option in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Chapter 9102.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Servicemember requires additional relief beyond what is currently available in the Guide,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers should contact their Freddie Mac representative.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8503.2: Applying and Servicing Servicemembers Civil
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Relief Act (SCRA)-capped Mortgages (08/13/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Applying SCRA provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Calculation of new monthly payment for SCRA-capped Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Application of payments on SCRA-capped Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Accounting reports for SCRA-capped Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Rate changes on SCRA-capped ARMs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing compensation for SCRA-capped Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Retroactive rate cap for SCRA-capped Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Applying SCRA provisions
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Interest rate cap
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Servicemember and the Mortgage qualify for the SCRA’s maximum interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: protection and the Servicemember has submitted a written request for protection in
elements.label: text

elements.text: accordance with the SCRA, Freddie Mac will:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a Servicemember who is a member of a reserve component, begin applying the
elements.label: text

elements.text: interest rate cap with the first monthly Mortgage payment due after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemember receives his or her orders
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a Servicemember who is a member of the uniformed services as defined by 10
elements.label: text

elements.text: U.S.C. 101(a)(5) or a Servicemember who belongs to the National Guard, begin
elements.label: text

elements.text: applying the interest rate cap with the first monthly Mortgage payment due after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting date or effective date of the Servicemember’s Period of Military Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reinstate the Mortgage to its contractual rate of interest beginning with the second
elements.label: text

elements.text: monthly Mortgage payment due one year after the date the Servicemember’s Period
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Military Service terminates
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Borrower is delinquent on his or her monthly payments, the Servicer must contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (see Directory 5) via Form 105, Multipurpose Loan Servicing Transmittal,
elements.label: text

elements.text: no later than five Business Days after receiving the request for interest rate relief.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For reinstatement purposes, the contractual rate of interest is defined as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For a fixed-rate Mortgage, the same rate that was in effect before the reporting date or
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date of Military Service, or the date the Servicemember received orders (for
elements.label: text

elements.text: members of a reserve component)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For an ARM, the rate to be determined or calculated based on the last interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: that would have been in effect, or the last payment adjustment that otherwise would
elements.label: text

elements.text: have taken place, during the rate cap period
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the interest rate cap request is made by the Servicemember in accordance with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: SCRA notice provisions and it is received by the Servicer no later than 180 days after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Period of Military Service terminates, the Servicer must apply the interest rate cap
elements.label: text

elements.text: retroactively to the first monthly Mortgage payment due after the reporting date,
elements.label: text

elements.text: effective date of Military Service or date the Servicemember received orders (for
elements.label: text

elements.text: members of a reserve component). However, Freddie Mac will bear the interest loss only
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the first monthly Mortgage payment due after the Funding Date.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Written notice to Servicemember, Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 15 Business Days of receiving an interest rate cap request on a Mortgage that
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets the requirements of the SCRA, the Servicer must provide written notice of
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval to the Servicemember or the Servicemember’s representative, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac does not prescribe a particular form of notice. The notice must contain the
elements.label: text

elements.text: following at a minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The amount of the monthly installment of principal and interest calculated at the rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: of 6% per year
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. The amount of the monthly installment of Escrow, if any, which remains unchanged
elements.label: text

elements.text: unless insurance coverage, property tax rates or other assessments are modified
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The total amount and first Due Date of the new monthly Mortgage payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. The reinstatement of the monthly Mortgage payment to its contractual rate of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: beginning with the second monthly payment due following the date that is one year
elements.label: text

elements.text: after the Servicemember is released from Military Service
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. If known at the time, the scheduled date the Servicemember will be released from
elements.label: text

elements.text: Military Service and the Due Date of the first monthly payment at the reinstated
elements.label: text

elements.text: contractual rate of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within 30 Business Days of receiving the request, the Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii) Assessing a Servicemember’s ability to pay at the Note Rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless there are unusual circumstances, Freddie Mac does not require the Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: assess the material effect of Military Service on a Borrower’s ability to continue making
elements.label: text

elements.text: payments on the Mortgage at the contractual rate of interest. The Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: implement the 6% rate cap, upon request of a Borrower, provided that the Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: meets the requirements of the SCRA.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemember’s ability to pay at the contractual rate of interest is not materially affected
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Directory 3) that Freddie Mac may wish to investigate the Servicemember’s
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Note

elements.label: text

elements.text: should be challenged. If so, Freddie Mac will provide the Servicer with instructions on
elements.label: text

elements.text: how to proceed.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: granted to a Servicemember or if the Mortgage did not qualify for SCRA protection (for
elements.label: text

elements.text: example, the Servicemember was already in a Period of Military Service at the time the

elements.text: Mortgage was originated). If Freddie Mac informs the Servicer that Freddie Mac declines
elements.label: text

elements.text: the request, then within five Business Days, the Servicer must advise the Servicemember
elements.label: text

elements.text: or the Servicemember’s representative in writing, citing the specific reasons the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage does not qualify for the interest rate cap and offer to consider other forms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: assistance.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If further guidance is required, the Servicer may contact its investor reporting specialist
elements.label: text

elements.text: (see Directory 3).

elements.text: (v) Foreclosure relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must not foreclose or initiate foreclosure proceedings against a Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: who is a Servicemember except in accordance with the SCRA and State Military Relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: Laws. See Sections 8503.1(b) and 8503.1(c) for Freddie Mac-specific foreclosure relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: in addition to that provided by the SCRA or State Military Relief Laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: When foreclosure is allowed to proceed and if a mortgage insurance claim is eventually
elements.label: text

elements.text: filed, the Servicer must notify the MI that the Mortgage was subject to SCRA or State
elements.label: text

elements.text: Military Relief Laws so that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest for SCRA-capped payments due, but unpaid by the Servicemember, is
elements.label: text

elements.text: claimable at the Note rate, not at the SCRA-capped rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Claimable interest will not be curtailed by the MI for a delay that was caused solely
elements.label: text

elements.text: by the Servicer’s compliance with applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: For further guidance on relief or remedies (other than interest rate cap under the SCRA)
elements.label: text

elements.text: involving Servicemembers who are serving or have served a Period of Military Service,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer must consult with their legal counsel or contact Freddie Mac (see Directory
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi) Late charges
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must waive all late charges or penalties resulting from Mortgage payments
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferred, restructured or rate-capped under the SCRA or if required by State Military
elements.label: text

elements.text: Relief Laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) Credit reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must report a Servicemember who is receiving the protection of the SCRA
elements.label: text

elements.text: or State Military Relief Laws as “paying as agreed” to the credit repositories.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must fully comply with the SCRA and State Military Relief Laws as they
elements.label: text

elements.text: relate to credit reports kept on Servicemembers who exercise their statutory rights to
elements.label: text

elements.text: military relief.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Maintaining correspondence and documentation
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must maintain records of all communications with, and copies of all
elements.label: text

elements.text: correspondence and documentation to or from, Servicemembers seeking Mortgage relief
elements.label: text

elements.text: for hardship caused by Military Service in the Mortgage file.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ix) Transfers of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Transfer of Servicing includes Mortgages that have any type of military relief,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including Mortgages with interest rates capped under the SCRA or with suspended
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosures, the Transferor must identify each such Mortgage to the Transferee before
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferring the Mortgage files to the Transferee. See Chapter 7101 for more information
elements.label: text

elements.text: on Transfers of Servicing.
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Period of Military Service and covered under the SCRA or foreclosure relief provided to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemembers and their Dependents in accordance with Section 8503.1(b). When
elements.label: text

elements.text: utilizing default action code 32, Servicers must provide the date default processes were
elements.label: text

elements.text: suspended and continue to report each month that the Servicemember is receiving
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure protection under the SCRA or Section 8503.1(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Default reason code 14 – “Military Service”: When reporting Mortgage relief, loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: modifications, or liquidation options extended to Servicemembers, Servicers must report
elements.label: text

elements.text: the default action code associated with the specific relief, modification, or liquidation
elements.label: text

elements.text: option. Servicers must also report the default reason code 14 to indicate the hardship was
elements.label: text

elements.text: caused by military service.

elements.text: (b) Calculation of new monthly payment for SCRA-capped Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the interest rate on a Mortgage is capped in accordance with Section 8503.2(a), the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer may choose between the following two methods for calculating the new monthly
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment amount:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Reamortization of the Mortgage based on the interest rate of 6%, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implementation of the 6% rate using the original amortization schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Revised interest rate, reamortized Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: This method applies in calculating the principal and interest (P&I) payment amount both
elements.label: text

elements.text: at:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The interest rate of 6%
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The reinstated contractual rate of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must use the following formula:
elements.label: text

elements.text: a = Mortgage UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. For 6% rate: scheduled UPB after application of the last payment made before the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Due Date of the first payment at 6%
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. For reinstatement to contractual rate: scheduled UPB after application of the last
elements.label: text

elements.text: payment made before the Due Date of the second payment due one year after the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemember’s Period of Military Service ends
elements.label: text

elements.text: b = interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Rate applicable to the rate cap period: 6%
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Rate applicable when reinstated to contractual rate: for fixed-rate Mortgages, use
elements.label: text

elements.text: the original Note rate; for ARMs, use the current applicable index plus margin
elements.label: text

elements.text: c = actual number of remaining payments, using a calculated term to maturity
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) 6% rate cap, original amortization schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: Under this method, the Servicer must calculate the Borrower’s monthly P&I at 6% as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: d + e = monthly P&I
elements.label: text

elements.text: d = scheduled principal as calculated per amortization schedule in effect before rate cap
elements.label: text

elements.text: e = accrued interest = current cycle Ending UPB x (.06/12)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon reinstatement to the contractual rate of interest, the P&I amount will correspond to
elements.label: text

elements.text: the amortization schedule in effect before the rate cap.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Application of payments on SCRA-capped Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Monthly payments made by Servicemembers on SCRA-capped Mortgages must be applied
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with Section 8103.3(a).
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Yield (ANY) rate. The Servicer will receive credit for the interest rate differential, which is
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Interest rate differential = ANY less adjusted interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adjusted interest rate or Mortgage with Note Rate higher than net yield = 6% less
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing fee paid to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Continue to report the contractual principal and interest payment in the monthly loan￾level transaction for each Mortgage subject to the SCRA interest rate reduction
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (g) Retroactive rate cap for SCRA-capped Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Retroactive application of the SCRA rate cap under Section 8503.2(a) must be processed as
elements.label: text

elements.text: follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Reimbursement to the Servicemember
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must calculate the difference between the amount of interest paid by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicemember at the contractual rate of interest and the amount of interest due at 6%,
elements.label: text

elements.text: for the applicable benefit period. At the Servicemember’s option, the resulting amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: may be credited to the UPB of the Mortgage or to reduce monthly Escrow installments,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or refunded to the Servicemember.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Reimbursement to the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicer must calculate the credit that results from the retroactive interest rate
elements.label: text

elements.text: differential and notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 3) of the amount of the credit the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer is claiming (see Section 8503.2(d)). Freddie Mac will validate the credit amount
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicer requests and enters it on the “Adjustment Line” of the Servicer’s MAS.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8504.1: Outreach and notification requirements for
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Mortgages with low interest-bearing UPB and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: deferred balance that are approaching the Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: maturity date or payoff of the interest-bearing UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Outreach requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Written notification requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: For Mortgages with an outstanding non-interest bearing UPB (or deferred balance) that is due at
elements.label: text

elements.text: the time of maturity or payoff of the interest-bearing UPB, Servicers must complete additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: outreach in accordance with the requirements below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers are reminded that for delinquent Borrowers, the Servicer must continue to follow all
elements.label: text

elements.text: existing Guide requirements, including collection efforts in order to achieve quality right party
elements.label: text

elements.text: contact and the loss mitigation evaluation hierarchy unless they conflict with the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: below.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Outreach requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: As early as 180 days but no later than 150 days before the Mortgage maturity date or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: projected payoff date of the interest-bearing UPB (the “projected payoff date”), the Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must take the following actions:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Provide written notice to the Borrower informing the Borrower that the non-interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: bearing UPB will become due and provide the projected payoff date or pending Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: maturity date, as applicable, and the amount of the non-interest bearing UPB
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon establishing contact with the Borrower, discuss with the Borrower that the non￾interest bearing UPB will become due on the projected payoff date or pending Mortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: maturity date and determine, based on communication with the Borrower, whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower is able to pay the non-interest bearing UPB on the maturity date or projected
elements.label: text

elements.text: payoff date
